programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates Louises cat Pip, a one-eyed cerebral hypoplasia feline, which will not stop tormenting and attacking the owners other cat, Red. Plus, Erins moggy Autumn causes havoc by urinating in several places around the home, including the settee, beds, kitchen worktops, microwave, computer monitor and backpacks

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps expectant parents Sara and John with their aggressive cat, Lucy. Plus, Keri wants to become an animal psychiatrist, but the daily fights between her two felines make her doubt her chosen career path

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: The wardens investigate a suspected poacher, while an alligator and her hatchlings must be captured and relocated from an East Texas school

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps Kristen, whose cat Moose makes a misery of her life by marking everything in her house. The expert works his techniques to transform its ways

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy helps a woman whose cat has not used a litter box for two years, and another troubled feline terrorises a fellow cat and its owner

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson returns to Texas to build a sophisticated treehouse in the heart of Dallas. He designs a clean-lined modern structure with a luxurious touch

programme.title: Meet the Superbrains
programme.desc: Two teams of scientists demonstrate why electricity could help end humanitys reliance on fossil fuels, and build an electric speedboat and plane to prove their point

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts answer more questions about the animal kingdom, including whether dogs feel guilty when they do something wrong and why tigers are orange

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Investigating what dogs see when they watch TV, how snakes eat prey twice the size of their heads, and how cuttlefish are able to vanish without a trace

programme.title: Extinct or Alive
programme.desc: In Indonesia, Forrest Galante seeks the Millers grizzled langur, or Dracula monkey. When he hears of an illegal pet market, his search becomes a rescue mission

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: The Browns bring a haul to Port Alexander, and poor weather becomes the least of their problems when the Integrity becomes stranded in the middle of the water

programme.desc: The pressure is on the team to finish transforming actor Tracy Morgans pool house into an aquarium big enough to house his sharks

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto surveys the condition of his rusting barge after a winter in drydock. Elsewhere, Eivin and Eve use explosives to clear land for a new root cellar

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: A broken-down bulldozer sends Jeremy on an airborne cross-country mission, and Jenny finds herself facing off with a fierce predator as she is stalked in the wild by a wolf

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Jeremy must enter an uneasy alliance with an old enemy as he makes plans to expand his farmstead, and Neil tackles some major upgrades as he prepares to sell the town

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: Specialists armed with the latest technology set out to locate the fabled bigfoot. The start of their search takes them to the forests of Oregon

programme.desc: Documentary in which former Olympic swimmer Michael Phelps tests his skills in the water by competing in a race against a shark - one of the worlds fastest marine predators

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: A game of underwater copycat shows how smart dolphins really are. Plus, how hyenas can eat pretty much anything and geckos ability to apparently defy gravity

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: How humpback whales are able to leap so far out of the water, why dogs tilt their heads and how do spiders shed their own skin. Plus, why do animals gather in swarms?

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: The Irwins caravan into the Outback to release baby kangaroos and an emu onto their wildlife conservation area - but ffind more animals in need of rescue

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Game Warden Mike Boone teams up with local police to search for armed suspects after a robbery. Plus, a family are investigated for illegally keeping a pet deer

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Thorpeness in Suffolk, the first purpose-built seaside village in the UK. The ingenuity of amenities such as the hand-dug lake, country club and inland housing were the unique vision of Glencairn Stuart Ogilvie. Hamish Ogilvie helps Ben to understand his great-grandfathers vision, stemming from theatrical inspiration

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter travels to north Wales where, more than 200 years ago, engineer Thomas Telford created a 126ft-high aqueduct that spans the vale of Llangollen. She finds out why it has become a World Heritage Site by taking a six-mile walk along the winding Dee Valley, finishing at the landmark Telford described as a stream through the skies

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 May 1999 and featuring Texas, Sugar Ray, Travis, Shania Twain, Sixpence None the Richer, Hepburn and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 June 1999 and featuring Sixpence None the Richer, The Wiseguys, Garbage, Chicane feat Maire Brennan, Supergrass, Shed Seven, Jamiroquai and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the chart show programme, first broadcast on 11 March 1982. Includes performances from Bow Wow Wow, Fun Boy Three and Bananarama, Depeche Mode, Julio Iglesias, Haircut 100, The Associates, Adrian Gurvitz and Tight Fit

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Mayo, Sybil Ruscoe and Rod McKenzie host the edition first shown on March 16, featuring performances by New Order, Gloria Estefan, Chanelle, Fuzzbox, Madonna, Soul II Soul with Caron Wheeler, Jason Donovan and Guns N Roses

programme.title: St Patricks Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A collection of classic pop performances from the BBC archives to celebrate St Patricks Day, reflecting the huge impact that music from Ireland and Northern Ireland has made across the globe. Featuring U2, Sinead OConnor, Van Morrison, the Undertones, Thin Lizzy, the Pogues, the Corrs, the Cranberries, Boyzone and Westlife

programme.title: Other Voices 2026: Dingle, Ireland
programme.desc: Annie Macmanus, Huw Stephens and MayKay present music from St James Church in Dingle, County Kerry, featuring performances by Dermot Kennedy, Amble, Travy, Florence Road, Jessy Blakemore, Dry Cleaning, Shame, Dove Ellis, Miles Kane, Wunderhorse and Jacob Alon

programme.title: Enya at the BBC
programme.desc: The Irish singer and composer as seen on the BBC, including her breakthrough single Orinoco Flow, which stayed at the top of the UK charts for three weeks in 1988. Since then she has released nine top 10 albums and won multiple awards all while maintaining her privacy, rarely giving interviews and never touring

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Jo Whiley introduces a 2016 performance by the singer from the BBC Radio Theatre in front of an intimate audience, featuring songs from his album Keep Me Singing

programme.title: Boomtown Rats: Sight and Sound in Concert
programme.desc: DJ Pete Drummond introduces a classic concert by Bob Geldofs band from the Goldiggers, then a popular nightclub and concert venue in Chippenham, Wiltshire. The programme was first shown in February 1984, the same year that the lead singer would go on to found the charity Band Aid, raising money to combat poverty in Ethiopia

programme.title: The Irish Rock Story: A Tale of Two Cities
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of how rock music evolved in Ireland over 40 years. The film demonstrates how the sounds that emerged were grounded in the musical traditions of the islands two main cities, from the pioneering touring showbands of the late 1950s through to the modern day. Richard Dormer narrates an examination of how the harder sounds of Belfast merged with the more folk-centric traditions of Dublin, celebrates some of the key musicians to have emerged from the Irish scene, and reveals how they were influenced by the political, social and cultural climate of their surroundings. With contributions from U2, Sinead OConnor and Bob Geldof

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 May 1999 and featuring Texas, Sugar Ray, Travis, Shania Twain, Sixpence None the Richer, Hepburn and Shanks and Bigfoot

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of an abandoned baby grey squirrel found by chance in the Oxfordshire countryside. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents the silver jubilee edition of the old-time music hall programme from January 1978, with Ken Dodd, Jan Hunt and members of the Players Theatre, London, taking to the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.sub-title: A Rose by Any Other Name
programme.desc: A middle-aged widows family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether hes a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: The Turkish Detective
programme.desc: When Ayse and Suleyman are sent to investigate a house fire, all seems innocuous at first, but they soon turn up a clue that may break new ground in Suleymans search for Kayra Khan. Theyre even more surprised when the person who blocks them investigating it is the chief inspector Ikmen

programme.title: The Turkish Detective
programme.desc: The team finally learns the truth about Kayra Khan, but bringing someone to justice will be tough on everyone, while Chief inspector Ikmen faces an uphill struggle to convince his superiors to commit to his plan. Crime drama, starring Haluk Bilginer and Ethan Kai

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.sub-title: Joanna Lumley, Ian McKellen, Neil Morrissey and Marion Montgomery
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson is joined by actress Joanna Lumley, actors Ian McKellen and Neil Morrissey and American jazz singer Marion Montgomery. First broadcast in 1999

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth feels harassed by a combination of working at a charity shop, trying to impress Councillor Nugent and worrying about Roses love life. To cap it all, Daisy informs her that a strange woman in Daddys bedroom claims to be his fiancee. Starring Patricia Routledge

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy takes his mothers advice when an insanely jealous man takes an interest in his girlfriend. Ronnie Corbett stars

programme.title: Nature and Us: A History Through Art
programme.desc: James Fox tells the story of the human races ever-changing relationship with nature through the lens of some of the worlds most extraordinary artworks, beginning with a look back at the art of the ancient world, looking at the art of prehistoric hunters, the advent of agriculture, the first cities, and the beginnings of Hinduism and Christianity

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.sub-title: A Rose by Any Other Name
programme.desc: A middle-aged widows family become convinced a photographer is only marrying her for money and ask Hetty to investigate. Given a week to probe his past and find out whether hes a gold-digging cad or a 24-carat gentleman, the unlikely sleuth unearths some extremely interesting facts. Drama based on the novel by David Cook, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents the silver jubilee edition of the old-time music hall programme from January 1978, with Ken Dodd, Jan Hunt and members of the Players Theatre, London, taking to the stage of the Leeds City Varieties

programme.title: Wildlife on One
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of an abandoned baby grey squirrel found by chance in the Oxfordshire countryside. First broadcast in 1981

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: A panel of BBC journalists hosts a weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering the usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts the award-winning comedy panel show. Lee Mack and David Mitchell are joined by guest panellists Nabil Abdulrashid, Alex James, Nella Rose and Lou Sanders, who reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: Appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Tommy Walsh spots his first sparkly staircase in a semi in Maidstone, Kent. He also follows a development of a two-bedroom forever home bungalow in Brightlingsea, Essex. In Alfreton, Derbyshire, Dion Dublin is impressed with the guide price of a smart terrace

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Detling antiques fair, in Kent. Catherine Southon helps mother-and-daughter duo Fiona and Millie on the red team while Jonathan Pratt guides married couple Carlie and Martin in blue as they search for bargains to sell at auction

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The Father is in a race against time to prove the innocence of a man currently standing trial for murder. Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The focus is firmly on Wiltshire, as Jonnie Irwin helps a couple from London with a £550,000 budget to make a life-changing rural move to this attractive county. Jonnie also meets a group of bicycling enthusiasts, joining them on a ride through the fascinating Longleat Estate

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk tackle a scale model of the Falkirk Wheel, the missing cogs, broken bearings and underperforming motor of which demand precision repairs. In the barn, Dom entrusts a soft toy to Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch for a sensitive rebuild, before Will swaps timber for silver, making a decorative bowl with celebrated silversmith Katie Watson

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Alistair, Kelly, George, Paula and Tamanna race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong and Ed Gamble

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The next group of contestants enter the world of pop-ups with dishes featuring tacos, octopus and custard tarts and hopes of impressing 25 movers and shakers from the pop-up world. One chef will soar straight into the semi-finals while the other four go on to cook a family favourite for judges Monica Galetti, Marcus Wareing and Matt Tebbutt

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Neglected Birds Found and Highland Blaze Seen from Space
programme.desc: An emergency in Hertfordshire uncovers a case of animal neglect and in the Scottish Highlands, fire crews battle a wildfire so large its visible from space. Paul Colgan takes part in first aid training catered specifically to farmers in Dumfries

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team investigate the murder of a local newspapers notorious agony aunt, poisoned the morning after her retirement party while reading her final advice column. Mervin resists the counselling sessions the Commissioner has imposed after the case in Antigua and gets a rude awakening from an unexpected figure

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: From Strictly to the sofa, Claudia kicks off her chat show which promises Hollywood actors, the best of British and comedy stars - all put at their ease by the hosts trademark warmth, charm and humour. Her first guests are Hollywood star and musician Jeff Goldblum, actress Vanessa Williams, comedy icon Jennifer Saunders and funnyman Tom Allen

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for an episode of previously unseen material. Among the guests are Bob Mortimer, Jo Brand, Yinka Bokinni, Jason Isaacs, Mark Chapman,, Alasdair Beckett-King, Gbemisola Ikumelo, Gyles Brandreth, Dianne Buswell, Jamelia, Tasha Ghouri, Josie Gibson, Harriet Kemsley, Jessica Knappett and Richard Osman

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: This week, the makeover challenge goes Olympic as the queens drag up a host of Team GB Olympians, including Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Paul Ainsworth, Jess Filbey and Tom Parker Bowles, and special guest Alex Kingston. Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Wiltshire has some great potential, including a quirky cupboard with a lovely view, although Martin Roberts is concerned about access to the outside space. Martel Maxwell is in Nottinghamshire, where a mixed commercial and residential unit has access issues of its own. In Greater Manchester, Dion Dublin thinks a three-bedroom semi is ripe for extension

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Alex Scott and guests look ahead to the weekends matches, including Chelsea v Newcastle United, Arsenal v Everton, and Manchester United v Aston Villa

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents the show from Shepton Mallet, Somerset, with the reds and blues led by John Cameron and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah tours Lincolnshire with a couple looking to relocate there from Scotland, showing them the very best property the county has to offer for their £220,000 budget

programme.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse (2018)
programme.desc: After being bitten by a radioactive arachnid, student Miles Morales develops incredible abilities, and becomes a version of Spider-Man. When a super collider device opens a gateway to other dimensions, Miles crosses paths with five counterparts to stop a threat for all realities. Oscar-winning animated adventure, with the voices of Shameik Moore and Nicolas Cage

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Italy (Kick-off 4.40pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of both teams fifth and final match of the championship, held at Principality Stadium. Wales lost their two previous home games against France and Scotland, meaning they have not won a Six Nations match here since 2022. They were also beaten 22-15 by Italy when the teams met at Stadio Olimpico in last years competition. With analysis from Sam Warburton, Martin Johnson, and Sergio Parisse, and commentary by Andrew Cotter, Jonathan Davies, and John Barclay

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh host the first of this years semi-finals. Over five gruelling events and the energy-draining Eliminator, only the fastest and strongest will make it to the grand final. The referees are on point in Collision, unbeaten Comet returns to Hang Tough, and Cyclone gets bored on top of Everest

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Siobhans trauma leads her to push Cam away, and she wrestles with a crisis of morality after putting pressure on Flynn to seek revenge on Chris Banfield for her. Jan tries to push Teddy to settle things with Jacob while treating a patient who took a deliberate overdose, and Kim tries to prove herself to a former tutor

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: This Wasnt the Plan. But Its Where We Are
programme.desc: Anna feels weighed down by emotions she cant release, while Rachel is unable to sleep and desperate to quiet her racing thoughts. Claire takes Kate to the doctor, learning she needs glasses, before scrambling to prepare a costume for a last-minute dress-up day. Maggie confides that she and Garv are trying for a baby, while Luke decides to visit Rachel in rehab

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Chelsea v Newcastle United at Stamford Bridge. City claimed a comfortable 3-0 win over the Hammers in Decembers reverse fixture, and Chelsea came back from 2-0 down at halt time to earn a 2-2 draw against the Magpies. Plus, Burnley v Bournemouth, Sunderland v Brighton and Hove Albion, and Arsenal v Everton

programme.title: The Football Interview
programme.desc: Kelly Somers has a conversation with Brentford and Republic of Ireland defender Nathan Collins

programme.title: Alan Partridge: Alpha Papa (2013)
programme.desc: The hapless local radio DJ ends up in the centre of a siege when colleague Pat Farrell gets fired by the stations new owners, only to respond by returning to the studio with a shotgun and taking hostages. The police enlist Alans aid as a negotiator, giving him the chance to revive his own flagging public profile. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Colm Meaney and Felicity Montagu

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including West Ham United v Manchester City at London Stadium and Chelsea v Newcastle United at Stamford Bridge. City claimed a comfortable 3-0 win over the Hammers in Decembers reverse fixture, and Chelsea came back from 2-0 down at halt time to earn a 2-2 draw against the Magpies. Plus, Burnley v Bournemouth, Sunderland v Brighton and Hove Albion, and Arsenal v Everton

programme.title: Sunday with Laura Kuenssberg
programme.desc: Interviews with key politicians and public figures

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: Seven well-known personalities with differing faiths and beliefs put on their backpacks and waterproofs and set out travel more than 1,600km on foot and by road and sea to learn about St Columba, a key figure in early British Christianity. Following ancient paths and heritage walking trails are interior designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, former England cricketer Monty Panesar, actress Louisa Clein, TV presenters Nick Hewer and Scarlett Moffatt, comedian Shazia Mirza and Paralympian Will Bayley

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommys road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldnt train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity - a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: Louis immerses himself in Floridas multimillion-dollar rap scene, which is as notable for the controversy surrounding its emerging stars as its creativity. He spends time with a number of artists who are achieving success through self-promotion on social media, from those playing out fierce rivalries online to rappers live-streaming their often violent and chaotic lifestyles. While drawing in an ever-growing fanbase attracted to its unvarnished portrayal of real-life struggles and hardship, this content has also drawn the attention of the authorities, who see it as evidence of criminals indulging in illegal gang activity

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mails delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but Johns worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Spider-Man 3 (2007)
programme.desc: A sinister alien parasite brings out the web-slinging heros darker side, filling him with thoughts of revenge on his uncles murderer, the shape-shifting Sandman. Meanwhile, he also has to contend with the challenge of his best friend following in his fathers villainous footsteps - and a jealous rival developing superpowers of his own. Superhero sequel, with Tobey Maguire, Kirsten Dunst and James Franco

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis Theroux spends time with singer-songwriter Rachel Keen at her home, backstage at Glastonbury and in a recording studio. She tells Louis about the challenges of balancing her ambitions as an artist with commercial demands and opens up about painful experiences shes suffered within the industry

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: As the rise of platforms like OnlyFans gives adult entertainment performers financial independence away from the studio model of old, Louis encounters a new generation using their online platforms to call out alleged predatory behaviour by prominent figures from the business. As a result, an industry that prides itself on pushing sexual boundaries is asking itself difficult questions about consent, and infamous figures like long-time performer Ron Jeremy are being challenged in court

programme.title: Escape Room (2019)
programme.desc: The prospect of winning a significant sum of money lures a group of individuals into a life-threatening scenario. Being locked in several rooms with extreme conditions, they discover the secrets behind the escape room and must fight to survive by finding a way out. Psychological thriller, starring Deborah Ann Woll, Taylor Russell and Tyler Labine

programme.title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions (2021)
programme.desc: The survivors of a deadly game designed by a sinister corporation are dragged back into an even deadlier trap. And as a handful of former escapees join in, the competitors must work together to outsmart their inhuman tormentors and give their all to solve increasingly sophisticated puzzles. Horror sequel, starring Taylor Russell and Logan Miller

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: As the rise of platforms like OnlyFans gives adult entertainment performers financial independence away from the studio model of old, Louis encounters a new generation using their online platforms to call out alleged predatory behaviour by prominent figures from the business. As a result, an industry that prides itself on pushing sexual boundaries is asking itself difficult questions about consent, and infamous figures like long-time performer Ron Jeremy are being challenged in court

programme.title: Living Every Second: The Kris Hallenga Story
programme.desc: Diagnosed with Stage IV breast cancer at 23, Kris Hallenga made a radical decision about what she wants to do with the rest of her life. Together with her twin sister, Maren, Kris founded the Coppafeel charity, harnessing the power of her own experience to show millions of young people how to check for signs of cancer. In 2013, Kris made a film about her life for the BBC and, over the decade that followed, continued to invite the cameras back. This film reveals how her energy, drive and ambition has left an important legacy

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Weeping Angels take over New York in both the present and the past. Rory finds himself drawn back in time to the 1930s, where River Song is playing private eye, and a harvest of human life is being planned. The Doctor and Amy rush to the rescue, only to discover a terrible future may already be written in stone. Alex Kingston and Mike McShane guest star in Karen Gillan and Arthur Darvills final episode

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctors search for Clara, the mysterious woman who died twice centuries apart, takes him to modern-day London and a run-in with a third incarnation. Something dangerous is lurking in the citys wi-fi signals, uploading peoples minds and imprisoning them - and among those targeted is the woman he has travelled through space and time to find. Jenna-Louise Coleman making her first official appearance alongside Celia Imrie

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mike and Alison welcome the very first guests to their new bijoux BandB. While serving the couple breakfast, Alison realises that they are not enjoying their break and, fearing a bad review, utilises the surprising skills of Lady Button and Julian to ensure the pair make the most of their weekend away. Thomas is perplexed yet secretly thrilled by the sudden attention from the Plaguers, who, after admiring Alisons discarded portrait of him are now affording him celebrity status in Button House, hanging on to his every word. Supernatural comedy, starring Charlotte Ritchie, Kiell Smith-Bynoe and Simon Farnaby

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Mary walks Julian through her journey of self-discovery and how she finally found her voice. Thomas realises enough is enough - he must go cold turkey on his Alison addiction and needs Pats undivided attention. Old friends of Mike check in to the guesthouse and agree to share the surprising secret of their success. The Captain is thrown by a change to his daily routine, so Kitty steps in to teach him how to live in the moment. Robin helps Lady Button to remember lost friends

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: Agnes needs someone to look after Grandad so she can treat herself to a much-needed holiday - but with private nursing homes being so expensive, it seems her only option is to convince the health visitor he has lost his marbles. Rory and Dino move in for a couple of weeks, and the matriarch is soon spitting feathers when the latter begins taking over the house, and her late husband gets in touch from beyond the grave with some ominous news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentines Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Bettys sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced strangers blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Musical extravaganza recalling the night when Janet met Jonny in Runcorn, told in flashback and accompanied by hits in the style of Christina Aguilera and Dr Dre. Viewers get to see the pairs first kiss as well as a chance to see how far Gaz will go to get Donna

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet tries to dump Jonny, but suffers the indignity of being upstaged by some pasties. Louise gets revenge on David, and Donna plucks up the courage to pursue a new dream. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Will Mellor, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny finds a new girlfriend, Gaz challenges David to a drinking contest, and a book has a profound effect on Louise. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny ruins his first date with Kate by trying to be too macho. A large spider spoils Louises plans to live with Janet, and Gaz tries to become a pop idol. Sitcom, starring Ralf Little, Natalie Casey, Will Mellor and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Lonely Janet and frisky Gaz grow closer than intended and Louise applies for a new job. Jonny and Kate learn how biscuits can spice things up in the bedroom. Comedy, starring Ralf Little, Kathryn Drysdale and Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers, flatmates and polar opposites Samir and Nikhil find themselves on an adventure when they accidentally steal a package from their maniac neighbour. Short, one-off comedy, starring Karan Gill, Ikky Kabir, Eshaan Akbar, Elina Saleh, Ed Kear and Andrea Valls

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara McDermott discovers the dark side of Thailands backpacker culture. In the first episode, she lands in trouble with the Thai police when she starts to uncover the scale of Thailands illegal sex industry. A visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law

programme.title: Jack the Ripper: The Case Reopened
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Emilia Fox and Professor of Criminology David Wilson re-examine the infamous murders, which took place in the Whitechapel district of London in the late 19th century. Using state-of-the-art technology, they hope to get closer than anyone has before to answering vital questions, including who Jack the Ripper was, what motivated his crimes, how he escaped justice, and whether he could have been responsible for even more brutal murders

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs representing central England serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. They include a duck breast and celeriac bread sauce celebrating the special effects team who worked on Inception, as well as an edible film reel with white chocolate and ginger mousse. Veteran judge Spencer Metzger helps to decide who deserves to stay and who should go. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Mervin stumbles across a crime scene when he goes in search of his brother Solomon on Antigua. In a remote cabin, a local businessman has been shot. But Solomon and his associate Cheech both swear the other is responsible, and theyre not letting Mervin go until each one has convinced him of their account

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: Its Not the Worst Thing Thats Happened This Week
programme.desc: Anna is in the hospital, reeling from events of the incident and desperate for answers. In rehab, Rachel is restless and increasingly agitated, struggling through withdrawal. Meanwhile, Maggie tells Garv she wants to start IVF, but he brushes her off, reluctantly agreeing to a rigid sex schedule

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Two members of a throuple attempt to work out their issues in the wild, taking on a raft-building task and an abseil and climbing challenge that pushes their communication to the limit. In a campfire chat, they find a way to explain their fears with the help of relationship therapist Simone. Will three people prove too many for this marriage to cope with?

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: In South Yorkshire, Will Kirk and Cindy Welland help restore a 19th-century grandfather clock for Doncaster School for the Deaf and in Birmingham, Dominic Chinea brings a classic coffee machine back to life for a family honouring their fathers legacy. Will Kirk discovers the delicate craft of marionette making

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive hits the road with a female Moroccan motorcycle gang and also visits the Juventus Academy to train with some of the countrys best young footballing talent. He goes antique shopping with an expert to haggle for some of Casablancas best bargains, and meets a Michelin-starred chef to grab the best morning catch to cook in his new fine-dining restaurant

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive concludes his journey by travelling to the northern tip of Morocco and meeting band the Master Musicians of Joujouka high in the Rif mountains. In a nearby farm, he grabs a bucket and helps milk goats to produce the smooth and creamy jben cheese. In the winding streets of Casablancas old town, he learns new skills from a lantern-making artisan and meets a local fashion influencer to find the perfect Moroccan kaftan.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: The journalist in conversation with people who influence our political thinking about what has shaped theirs

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which players compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best able to answer questions. Susan Calman hosts

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway hops across the water from Tenby to Caldey Island, which is known for its monastery, monks and lavender perfume, but is also ideal for a sunny stroll

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford explores how planners and architects took to Scotlands skies after the Second World War as they embarked on missions to redesign towns and cities. From building a motorway right through the heart of Glasgow, to one of Scotlands first tower blocks, James reveals how these utopian and controversial visions have transformed lives of people living in Scotland today

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The experts return to Glamis Castle, where they value a pair of Chinese Imperial bowls, a collection of more than 800 handbag mirrors, and a necklace sporting 2,000-year-old gems. Michael Aspel introduces highlights of other shows and explores the Queen Mothers childhood at this historic Scottish retreat

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and David Fletcher view antiques brought in by members of the public to the Dorford Centre in Dorchester. Among the items they appraise are a pin cushion doll, a Royal Doulton Black Jack and a globe, which turns out to be the days star item. Presenter Paul Martin looks at the geology of Dorsets Chesil beach

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills on double points Friday before the overall victor is revealed

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the final day of this weeks quiz as the action arrives in Bangor. TV presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peters Abby Cook return, and Paul Gorton resumes his role of unreliable tour guide, sharing stories, surprises and suspect claims as the overall winner is revealed

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The Shannon, which runs through the heart of Ireland, is the longest river in the British Isles. For locally born naturalist and cameraman Colin Stafford-Johnson, it is also the wildest. Here Colin paddles along the Shannon to reveal the creatures living in the water and along the banks, such as kingfishers, predatory pike and native red squirrels

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don kicks off the horticultural year by cutting back ornamental grasses, planting summer-fruiting raspberries and potting up dahlias. Adam Frost has moved house, and reveals his new garden. And Arit Anderson gets access behind the scenes at the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, where they do vital work in conserving endangered plants from all over the world

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Abandoned by their dominant males, the Xudum pride comes under siege from more intruder lions, and the mums with young cubs must link up with the rest of the pride for protection. Leopard mum Lediba struggles after a lion steals her kill. To feed her cub, she is forced to make a dangerous move. Pobe is teaching cheetah cubs Kea and Bo to hunt, but when a hyena pack arrives, their survival instincts are pushed to the limit. With predators all around, every mother must fight harder than ever to keep her young alive

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Host Tez Ilyas welcomes Spring Day and Ignacio Lopez to the stage at Londons Hammersmith Apollo where they each offer their unique views of life

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Matt Chorley

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Girl (2023)
programme.desc: A mother and her daughter confined to their isolated world they have obsessively created to protect themselves. Drama, starring Deborah Lukumuena, Danny Sapani and LeShantey Bonsu

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: At War: Trump, Israel and Iran - Panorama
programme.desc: Jane Corbin investigates the origins and aims of the US/Israeli offensive against Iran, and the future of the relationship between the UK and the US in light of Keir Starmers refusal to allow the use of UK bases for the first attacks. The programme asks what the campaign is achieving, how far it could escalate, and what it means for ordinary people caught in the fighting

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Veronica must decide her future now she is not currently a nun, but not fully part of the world either. The Maternity Home prepares to close its doors, but Dr Turner is determined to preserve equipment and resources for his patients until the last possible moment. The Mullucks fight for the rights of all Thalidomide victims, while Cyril and Rosalind prepare for their wedding

And Arit Anderson gets access behind the scenes at the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, where they do vital work in conserving endangered plants from all over the world

programme.title: Goodbye, Mr Chips (1969)
programme.desc: A shy, retiring schoolmaster sets out to woo a singing showgirl. He doesnt approve of her profession, but she finds life as an on-campus housewife hard to adjust to. Romantic musical based on James Hiltons novel, starring Peter OToole, Petula Clark, Michael Redgrave, George Baker and Sian Phillips

programme.title: Sweet Charity (1969)
programme.desc: A lonely but optimistic dancer in a seedy nightclub goes in search of true love, but finds instead a succession of deceitful men who only let her down. When she meets a shy, claustrophobic man in a lift, she thinks he might be the one - but tries to keep her past a secret. Musical, with Shirley MacLaine, John McMartin, Ricardo Montalban and Sammy Davis Jr, and featuring songs including The Rhythm of Life and Big Spender

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs representing central England serve their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry, with Michelin-starred chef Spencer Metzger as this weeks veteran judge. Highlights include a Jerusalem artichoke and turnip dish linked to War Horse and a monochrome, art-deco styled monkfish dish that pays homage to Birminghams Electric Theatre. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs representing central England serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. They include a duck breast and celeriac bread sauce celebrating the special effects team who worked on Inception, as well as an edible film reel with white chocolate and ginger mousse. Veteran judge Spencer Metzger helps to decide who deserves to stay and who should go. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the results and this afternoons news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Host Vernon Kay and unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on their knowledge of the UK, beginning with Belfast

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts Will Axon and Thomas Plant asses items in a visit Southend-on-Sea, with presenter Paul Martin meeting the son of a man who launched trampolining in Essex in the 1950s

programme.title: Jane Reas Bloomin Wild
programme.desc: It is winter in Shilnavogie, and the daily routine of feeding animals and keeping the farm tidy is easier said than done now that Jane is seven months pregnant. In the polytunnel, Jane is already thinking ahead to next year, where her visit to Glenarm Castle Walled Garden in the spring has inspired her to start a tulip patch of her own. She is also on a mission to save her dwindling flock of Jacob sheep

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.sub-title: Neglected Birds Found and Highland Blaze Seen from Space
programme.desc: An emergency in Hertfordshire uncovers a case of animal neglect and in the Scottish Highlands, fire crews battle a wildfire so large its visible from space. Paul Colgan takes part in first aid training catered specifically to farmers in Dumfries

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Music veterans and old friends Francis Rossi and Linda Nolan join experts Philip Serrell and Natasha Raskin Sharp on an antiques pleasure cruise around Surrey and Sussex. Its fitting they have an iconic 1970s motor to nip around in and £400 each to spend on some antique delights they both hope will make big profits at the Worcestershire auction

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: Katya begins in Germanys Bavarian mountains, paragliding over Neuschwanstein Castle before travelling to meet the mayor of Kallstadt, the ancestral home of the Trump family, and heading to Ramstein, Americas largest airbase in Europe Ramstein. Crossing to Spain, Katya begins in the region of Catalonia, discovering how its strong nationalist identity ties into the legacy of dictator Francisco Franco

programme.title: Liza: A Truly Terrific Absolutely True Story (2024)
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on actress and singer Liza Minnellis life during the 1970s, just after the death of her mother, when she confronted a range of personal and professional challenges. It was a period that saw her seek out a number of extraordinary mentors - Kay Thompson, Fred Ebb, Charles Aznavour, Halston and Bob Fosse. They all helped her to transform from a performer with boundless raw talent and famous parents to a mature, polished superstar of stage and screen

programme.title: Cabaret (1972)
programme.desc: A flighty nightclub singer in 1930s Berlin is drawn into a love triangle with a sensitive English writer and an aristocratic German, as the Nazi party grows in might and the country heads inexorably toward war. Oscar-winning musical directed and choreographed by Bob Fosse, starring Liza Minnelli, Joel Grey, Michael York and Helmut Griem

programme.title: New York, New York (1977)
programme.desc: An egotistical saxophonist starts a turbulent love affair with an up-and-coming vocalist at the end of World War Two as they both struggle to make it in the Big Band era. Martin Scorseses musical drama, starring Robert De Niro, Liza Minnelli, Lionel Stander, Barry Primus and Georgie Auld

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The reetective drama, starring Holliday Grainger. Whistleblower Rachel Carey is ready to launch a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. With the home secretary Isaac Turners support, the changes signal a new dawn in UK counter-terrorism policing, at least until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Vick Hope explore sugar beet harvesting in the east of England, 100 years after sugar was first grown in this country. In Norfolk, Vick meets farmers racing to lift the last of this years crop, while Charlotte visits one of the countrys biggest processing plants. Plus, Tom Heap investigates whats really in the sewage sludge spread as fertiliser

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major head to the West Lothian town of Bathgate, which has an incredible industrial past and now a popular commuter area for people working in Edinburgh. Ian helps out in an award-winning butchers and offers to do some taste testing, then works it off by climbing a hill to find some neolithic remains. He investigates the towns historic theatre and meets a local brass band before getting the chance to relax in Scotlands biggest floatation centre

programme.title: The Chase
