programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Manchester, Gillian and husband Steve kick things off with a simple menu and put the emphasis on presentation. Day two sees Holly and Richard lay on a vegetarian meal, which fails to please Gillian. Foodies Jen and Alex host the final night, featuring some rap poetry

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples based in Manchester take part in the dinner party competition. First up are Raf and Kevin with their healthy menu - unless you include the chocolate cake for dessert, that is. Their flamboyant apartment impresses Chantel and Stephen, but American Matthew seems less impressed. Day two sees Chantel and Stephen hosting a backyard party, while expectations are high on the final evening as food connoisseur Matthew and his wife Jane present an Asian-inspired menu

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Laid-back south Londoner Daniel wants to get his wedding grooving like a Caribbean street carnival. However, sensible student bride Sabrina dreams of a more traditional occasion and a wedding her parents will be proud of. While Sabrina pictures a tranquil location, Daniel splashes the cash on trucks and sound systems

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Beauty queen and bride-to-be Yasmin has her heart set on a fairy-tale wedding. Powerlifter Yiannis is taking Yasmins dream to the extreme, but will she be the belle of the ball, or will the nuptials have an ugly twist in the tale?

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Beauty therapist Maria is a perfectionist, placing her dream of a country house wedding in the hands of her prankster fiance, Sam

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Mothers boy Tiago plans to put his self-conscious bride Karlette on the big screen at his 1950s drive-in wedding. However, his attention-shunning fiancee wants her wedding day to be something intimate and contemporary

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Competitive adrenaline junkie Mark wants to fly high on his big day and has an adventurous plan to take his wedding to the sky. However, his straight-talking bride-to-be Lynsey is much more down to earth and has a very clear idea of the modest and intimate wedding she wants. While Mark aims to go above and beyond to make his wedding First Class, no nonsense Lynsey just hopes he is thinking about her first and foremost

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Mitchell Modell, CEO of Modells Sporting Goods, goes incognito among his employees. He poses as a contestant on a fake reality TV show, and tries learn more about the people working for his company, which has been in his family for four generations since its founding by his great-grandfather in 1889

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Kat Cole goes undercover among the workforce of her company Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from more than 900 bakeries in 40 countries worldwide. Kat has come a long way after being raised by a single mother who fed her family of three girls on $10 a week. She seeks advice from her old boss, former Hooters CEO Coby Brooks, who also went undercover, in his own organisation. Kat has some lofty goals for Cinnabon to become a worldwide food brand, and wants to make sure that the new products she has put in place are working before she implements her plans

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks in at the 90-year-old Monticello Hotel in Longview, Washington, where owner Phillip Lovingfoss has an apparent liking for alcohol that some of his staff claim is threatening to destroy the business. The challenge of turning around the hotels fortunes only increases when the chef sees shocking allegations about Phillip splashed all over the front page of the local newspaper

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: In Oregon, Gordon Ramsay tries to rescue the Applegate River Lodge, which is around $1million in debt. The chef questions whether owner Richard Daviss interest in smoking cannabis is distracting him from the problems at this family-built inn, and encounters Richards sons Duke and Dusty, whose fiery relationship seems to prevent them from working together to tackle the establishments financial troubles

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Starkville, Mississippi, to try to rescue the failing Hotel Chester. Despite having more than 40 years experience in the business, owner David Mollendor is at a loss as to how to make his dream of owning his own hotel work, while his wife Sukie is struggling as its chef, tackling the role without any formal catering experience. Gordon discovers a family tragedy that explains why this couple have become trapped in their business and wrestling with debt

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits a dog-friendly hotel in West Dover, Vermont, that is in urgent need of help. Owner Sandy MacDougall runs the Four Seasons Inn like a hostel, with unpaid staff staying in the rooms instead of paying guests. The acerbic chef aims to get the business back on track before Sandy loses everything, but wonders whether it would be kinder to simply put this dog-friendly hotel to sleep

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay checks in to Murphys Historic Hotel near Sacramento in northern California, where owners Brian, Kevin and Joel are often too distracted by their lively bar to look after their business. The national landmark, which has played host to an array of famous names including Mark Twain, President Ulysses S Grant and JP Morgan, has no dedicated accommodation manager, so guests sometimes struggle to receive the attention they need and end up feeling the hotel has become an afterthought beside the noisy saloon

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, as Roisin Conaty and Phil Wang compete against Joe Wilkinson and Lou Sanders. Regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley and lexicographer Susie Dent are on hand to adjudicate, while David ODoherty joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts hosts the comedy game show, with Richard Ayoade and Harriet Dyer taking on Bob Mortimer and Tom Allen. Regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley and lexicographer Susie Dent are on hand to adjudicate, Joe Wilkinson drops in and Ian Smith joins Susie in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Bill Bailey: Limboland
programme.desc: The comedian muses on peoples expectations of happiness in a performance recorded at the Hammersmith Apollo. He shares the story of a disastrous family trip to see the Northern Lights, recalls a meeting with Paul McCartney that did not go as expected and performs a heart-rending country and western ballad on the nature of love

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Bernard and Manny feel uncomfortable around their new, oddly sensual cleaner and decide to accept a house-sitting job to get away from him. Meanwhile, Fran goes on date with an available, attractive, charming man. Comedy, starring Dylan Moran, Bill Bailey and Tamsin Greig, with Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Bernard gets snubbed by the people who invited him to a dinner party the night before, making him wonder just how badly he behaved, and Manny turns crimefighter after he chases a bag-snatcher and is mistaken for a police officer. Comedy, starring Dylan Moran, Bill Bailey and Tamsin Greig

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr is joined by captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson for highlights from the latest series of the comedy panel show. A host of well-known faces make up the teams as they discuss the topical events that set the publics chins wagging

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts an edition of the irreverent panel show celebrating the Queens Diamond Jubilee. Team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by actor Miles Jupp, broadcaster Chris Kamara, former royal correspondent Jennie Bond and comedienne Sarah Millican to give their unique take on the royal weekend, while dissecting the regal opinion polls, surveys and statistics thrown their way

programme.title: Black Books
programme.desc: Bernard and Manny feel uncomfortable around their new, oddly sensual cleaner and decide to accept a house-sitting job to get away from him. Meanwhile, Fran goes on date with an available, attractive, charming man. Comedy, starring Dylan Moran, Bill Bailey and Tamsin Greig, with Kevin Eldon

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Tom plans a wedding at his favourite theme park, but his down-to-earth fiancee Becca has always dreamed of being treated like a princess on her special day

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Essex groom Gary plans an underwater wedding 200 miles from home, but his bride-to-be Lauren dreams of celebrating her big day in the Home Counties

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Craig decides to plan an Oktoberfest-themed wedding, but his uncompromising fiancee Sofia dreams of a boho beach wedding day

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Stephen J Cloobeck, chairman and CEO of hotel company Diamond Resorts International, becomes the first boss to go incognito at his own organisation for a second time. His mission is to rebuild a business that he has just purchased, Pacific Monarch Resorts, which has locations across the western US and Mexico. He disguises himself as an ex-construction worker who wants to open a boutique hotel, going the extra mile by having his ears pierced to complete the look

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.sub-title: Best Naughty Bits: The Good, the Bad and the Kinky
programme.desc: Another romp through the Naked Attraction archive, this time focusing on the people doing the choosing - from those that it seemed butter would not melt to the ones who revealed their darkest secrets. Highlights include the polyamorous couple picking a date to the time the bodies were covered in whipped cream, strawberries and custard

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Chris is forced to admit that Brook is ticking all of his boxes, Gia weighs up taking an intimate step with Scott after years of celibacy, while Alissa and David make the most of their mountain escape

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions to In With a Shout, Gordon Ramsays Future Food Stars, Rise and Fall, Saving Our Wild Isles, Last Woman on Earth with Sarah Pascoe, Obsession and Dr Down Below. First shown in April 2023

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica is jealous of Barrys outstanding performance when they both get after-school jobs at Spencers Gifts selling novelty presents at the shopping mall. Murray insists that Adam must get a job too, but Beverly thinks he should focus on his filmmaking instead

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam tries to develop an acting career, a talent agent decides hes only suitable for nerd roles, so Beverly makes it her mission to turn him into a teen heartthrob. Barry decides to embrace the punk rock lifestyle, and Murray attempts to get more involved in Ericas life

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Inspired by the Karate Kid films, Adam and the other Adam Goldberg decide to face off in a karate match to settle their long-running feud once and for all. Later, Barry and Adam ask Uncle Marvin for some martial arts training - but the only thing they learn is how to do his cleaning

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Jai and Leanne, best man Pells and father of the bride Bruce look back at their unforgettable sky high wedding in 2016

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch, Cam and Phil crash Jays annual hiking trip to find an elusive eagle after Gloria asks them to keep an eye on him, while Alex, Claire and Haley plan a baby shower

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil feels emasculated when Bill wows Dylan and Luke with heroic tales. He gets them over for some male bonding, but it goes rather wrong

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Comedy clips showcasing amusing internet videos on the themes of food and animals

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Los Angeles to help La Frite, an outdated French restaurant that is failing to make any profits. His first step is to try to reconcile the feuding family members who run the business, and so devises a plan to bring them together by using secret filming

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and his friends are recruited to the William Penn Academy baseball team. Barry remains in denial about his pitching skills, while Geoffs father interferes in his relationship with Erica

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Assisted by Geoff, Erica and Valley Erica find a new band member to replace Lainey, but Barry convinces Geoff that the new musician is trying to steal Erica from him. Adam and Murray each plot to get out of going to Valentines Day dinner with Beverly

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Alexs new company puts her up at a new luxury apartment with full amenities - and a list of high-profile residents that everyone is eager to take advantage of, without her knowing. Phil is on a mission to review the restaurants famous sliders for his food blog, while Mitch and Cam sneak in separately to meet David Beckham. Meanwhile, Luke and Manny head straight for the gym to try to pick up older women and end up in hot water. Guest starring Courtney Cox and Stephen Merchant

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil pays a visit to his dad to make sure he is okay after hearing some concerning news about him. Meanwhile, Jay gives Claire and Mitch some of their old childhood memorabilia, which prompts them to revaluate a long-held memory of their first family vacation

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer rent an apartment in an affluent area so their children can have a good education when Springfield Elementary is rated the worst school in the state. Bart wastes no time in establishing a bad-boy persona, and increases Lisas popularity by telling people she knows a pop star. With the voice of Elliot Page (Juno)

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Four Great Women and a Manicure
programme.desc: Marge takes Lisa for her first manicure at the salon, where they debate whether a woman can be smart, beautiful and powerful all at the same time. Four tales featuring strong women from history and literature are retold with Selma appearing as Elizabeth I, Lisa as Snow White, Marge as Lady Macbeth and Maggie as Howard Roark from The Fountainhead

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An outbreak of food poisoning at Krusty Burger is traced to barley produced in a nearby town of Norwegian immigrants and results in the mills being shut down, so the out-of-work residents venture to Springfield to find new jobs. However, when xenophobia starts to spread, Mayor Quimby decides to shut the borders

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Pasadena, California, where he visits Jamaican-themed restaurant Cafe Kingston. The business in run by Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works at a radiotherapy clinic next door, and is determined to introduce hospital-style techniques to the kitchen by sealing everything in plastic bags

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Eagle Rock in California to try to change the fortunes of Capri, a grubby restaurant that often remains closed during lunchtime because of the twin owners lazy attitude. A crisis ensues as soon as Gordon instructs the duo to clean the place, revealing that only tough measures are likely to save the business

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer helps Ned build a Christian amusement park called Praiseland as a lasting monument to Maude Flanders. Unfortunately, it meets with an unenthusiastic response - until a seemingly genuine miracle occurs

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer, Marge and the kids hitch a ride on a train, where they are entertained by a tramp who tells them a series of colourful yarns based on American folklore - all of which feature characters suspiciously reminiscent of Homer

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Manny takes time out to play with Luke, before having a heart-to-heart with stepsister Claire. Gloria goes shopping for a dress with Alex, and Phil urges Jay to fly his new model aeroplane with him. Comedy, starring Ed ONeill and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitchells mother tries to make amends for the incident at Jay and Glorias wedding but no one is happy to see her, and the whole family has dinner at Claires house. American comedy, guest starring Shelley Long

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: It is the end of the first week on the coach, and tour guide Brendan has a twist up his sleeve for tonights vote. An argument during a rowing race leaves one couple in jeopardy

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Kill Gil, Volumes I and II
programme.desc: Department store Santa Gil grants Lisas Christmas wish, but loses his job as a result. Feeling guilty, the family offers him a bed for the night - but he promptly moves in, along with all of his belongings. As the uninvited lodger becomes a pest, Marge looks for ways to get rid of him

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family goes away to Marges favourite childhood holiday destination, which has unfortunately changed since she was last there. Homer spurs the locals into sprucing the place up, but an exuberant firework display wrecks everything, forcing him to make amends by becoming a fisherman

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe is faced with a difficult choice when Evergreen Terrace develops a traffic problem

programme.title: Married at First Sight UK
programme.desc: Familiar faces return for a dinner party where some celebrate romance, but others face old tensions. One husband confronts his ex-wife over a shocking revelation, while a former bride finds herself in the eye of the storm as shes pulled into the centre of another couples conflict, sparking a fiery showdown

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The latest run kicks off with stag and hen nights, as a fresh group of hopeful singles take their final steps toward marrying strangers

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The weddings erupt into chaos when Luke forgets the rings, leaving Mel struggling to move past a rocky start and setting an awkward tone for their reception

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The honeymoons highlight stark contrasts as Gia and Scott wake up smitten and excited for tropical Townsville, while Mel and Luke remain distant, heading to a cold and uncomfortable getaway

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Chris is forced to admit that Brook is ticking all of his boxes, Gia weighs up taking an intimate step with Scott after years of celibacy, while Alissa and David make the most of their mountain escape

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barrys membership of a friendship group is jeopardised and a family swear jar is introduced. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: After Barry starts checking out the best medical schools, Lainey reveals she has won a scholarship to art college. Erica and Adam start to suspect that Murray loves the family dog Lucky more than them

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: High school lovebirds Maria and Ryan, his best man Tavis and Marias sister Veronica are together again on camera for the first time since their school-themed wedding in 2016

programme.title: Lego Masters Australia
programme.desc: Brand new: The teams are each given one of the five best challenges from previous seasons, competing not against each other, but instead against the team that won the task

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Claire help out with their childrens school projects, while Gloria and Cameron spend quality time together. Elsewhere, Mitchell and Manny learn they have more in common than they thought during a trip to watch a meteor shower with Jay. Comedy, starring Ty Burrell and Julie Bowen

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eaterys decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brothers trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to New Orleans to visit Zekes, a much-loved restaurant that has seen standards slip since the original owner died. The new proprietors, husband and wife duo Darryl and Ellen Cortello, focused all the efforts on the financial side of things, so it is up to Gordon to show them that they first need to love food to make any profit

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three of the restaurants he tried to save, returning to Lido, Anna Vincenzos and Le Bistro to find out whether his advice has been put to good use

programme.desc: The weddings erupt into chaos when Luke forgets the rings, leaving Mel struggling to move past a rocky start and setting an awkward tone for their reception

programme.title: Kindergarten Cop (1990)
programme.desc: A tough detective is forced to pose as a preschool teacher in a bid to capture a drug dealer whose son is one of the pupils. However, his biggest challenge turns out to be controlling a class full of five-year-olds. Comedy, starring Arnold Schwarzenegger, Penelope Ann Miller, Linda Hunt, Pamela Reed and Richard Tyson

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Boy Who Knew Too Much
programme.desc: Mayor Quimbys nephew is wrongly accused of assault, presenting Bart with a dilemma - if he volunteers the information that would clear the lads name, he risks being expelled from school for playing truant

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The family celebrates Maggies birthday. Bart steals Homers wallet to pay for an Itchy and Scratchy souvenir, and Grandma Bouvier is swept off her feet by Grampa - but Mr Burns provides romantic competition, convinced she is the woman for him

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The City of New York v Homer Simpson
programme.desc: Barney borrows Homers car and vanishes on a drunken binge. Weeks later, Homer gets a letter from New York saying the vehicle is illegally parked between the towers of the World Trade Centre. Although Homer hates the Big Apple - owing to an unfortunate incident from his youth - the family heads to there to try to get the car back

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: As Principal Skinner prepares to enjoy a ceremony celebrating his 20th year as head of Springfield Elementary, a stranger exposes him as a former street punk who stole his identity. Featuring the guest voice of Martin Sheen

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After making a mess of a speech at work, Homer tries to bolster his public speaking skills by turning to improvisational comedy. Marge decides to upgrade Barts tumbledown treehouse, but tensions arise when Bart questions her building skills

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge receives a jail sentence for child neglect after letting Bart go to the park unsupervised. While prison serves as a welcome break from her hectic home life, Homer struggles to hold the fort in her absence

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Babatunde Aleshe, Mo Gilligan, Andi Oliver, Rylan, Chris Packham, Shaun Ryder, Bez, Jane McDonald, Sara Cox, Annie Mac, Stephen Mangan, Gyles Brandreth, Lulu, Judi Love, Charlene White, Clare Balding, Alice Arnold and Bobby and Jeff Brazier review shows including Love Island, Bake Off: The Professionals, The Supervet, MILF Manor, Under Paris, The Motorway Hotel and Countdown

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on The Celebrity Traitors, Trigger Point, Michael McIntyres The Wheel, The Great British Bake Off, Love Is Blind, DIY SOS and BBC News (clocks)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their views of The Celebrity Traitors, Game of Wool: Britains Best Knitter, Trigger Point, Hunted, Squid Game: The Challenge, Hamzas Hidden Wild Isles and ITV News coverage of the diminution of Prince Andrew to Andrew Mountbatten Windsor

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: North London charmer Ori gets to know primary school teacher and judo black belt Nadia, south London hairdresser Mula is left waiting for her date to turn up, and singer-songwriter Dan aims to impress English student Nathania. Geordie postie Jodie hopes her date with Laura will be first-class, while retired engineer Eric meets part-time actress Jan

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Mula, 23, was stood up on her last visit to the restaurant, and shes hoping round two will be better. Funnyman AJ, also returning, tries to seduce her with his best jokes. Could it be second time lucky? Geordie postie Jodie is back after being friend-zoned last week, and shes pleasantly surprised by Irish beauty Louise. Tom is a suave, confident restaurateur looking for a refined lady, but dance teacher Alliee isnt immediately impressed by his well-rehearsed advances, including petrol-station flowers and champagne

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claires newfound positivity leads her to inadvertently approve of Lukes new relationship with someone from work, while Phil gets a shock when he surprises Alex at school

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Haley and Dylans plan to quietly elope before the babies are born doesnt go as smoothly as they had hoped. Meanwhile, Jay continues to try to get his new business off the ground by courting an Instagram celebrity

programme.title: My Spy (2020)
programme.desc: Special Forces operative turned spy JJ undertakes covert surveillance of Kate Newton, the sister-in-law of a terrorist. JJ is ordered to keep a low profile. Unfortunately, Kates resourceful nine-year-old daughter Sophie blows his cover and blackmails JJ into training her as a spy. Comedy, starring Dave Bautista and Chloe Coleman

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer present the show on E4 for the second week running to make way for the Winter Paralympics. Andi Osho has the lowdown on her new book, Maisie Adam and Sam Campbell preview the new series of Last One Laughing, Squeeze frontman Glenn Tilbrook chats about his music and Lucinda Light has gossip from Married at First Sight. Plus, music by Don Broco

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to the house of the founding father of Impressionism, Claude Monet, in Giverny, where he wastes no time in walking over the famous Japanese bridge to gaze at the water lillies. With his tour of the iconic garden complete, James sets up his mobile kitchen to cook a dish of crab and dulse, inspired by Monets need to provide large meals for the brood of eight children he had with his second wife. He also takes a peek inside Monets kitchen, and samples a pike dish served up by local chef Eric Guerin. A trip to a nearby orchard affords James the chance to prepare apple doughnuts, before he returns hope to cook Normandy tart - a local pudding - and an inventive omelette

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: This time, the chef visits the capital city, Paris, exploring one of the largest wholesale food markets in Europe, before setting up a cooking station in one of the warehouses to cook a dish using chicken and mushrooms. James also visits the kitchen of the world-renowned chef, Pierre Gagnaire

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of Sardinia, starting out in Cagliari, where everyone is in high spirits for the feast of St Efisio. The bikers join in by cooking a local speciality, almond and orange cakes. They also meet the tuna fishermen of Carloforte and a chef who has a special way of serving up platters of the fish, are shown how to make sheeps black pudding by a group of shepherds and are served a stew by the King of Sardinia

programme.desc: The quarter-final of the competition sees the remaining amateur bakers tackle biscuit-based challenges. programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to the house of the founding father of Impressionism, Claude Monet, in Giverny, where he wastes no time in walking over the famous Japanese bridge to gaze at the water lillies. With his tour of the iconic garden complete, James sets up his mobile kitchen to cook a dish of crab and dulse, inspired by Monets need to provide large meals for the brood of eight children he had with his second wife. He also takes a peek inside Monets kitchen, and samples a pike dish served up by local chef Eric Guerin. A trip to a nearby orchard affords James the chance to prepare apple doughnuts, before he returns hope to cook Normandy tart - a local pudding - and an inventive omelette

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: This time, the chef visits the capital city, Paris, exploring one of the largest wholesale food markets in Europe, before setting up a cooking station in one of the warehouses to cook a dish using chicken and mushrooms. James also visits the kitchen of the world-renowned chef, Pierre Gagnaire

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mary Ellen announces she is engaged to a fellow student Dr David Spencer and they start planning the wedding. However, her hasty marriage is threatened by her feelings for new town doctor Curtis Willard, and she begins to have second thoughts

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tom and Deborah head off for a well-earned holiday, but their hopes of getting away from it all are dashed when they realise that Len and Linda are staying at a campsite nearby. Back in Ormston, Arthur is unexpectedly called away from his duties in the village, and the impending hospital visit by fiery local councillor Eugenia Maddox (guest star Frances de la Tour) prompts the residents to rally round to make a good impression

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Most TV characters are incapable of straying very far from their native haunts without either finding romance or stumbling over a murder and Dr Finlay is no exception. Here, his much-needed Highland walking holiday ends in the former, but it still turns out to be pretty hectic. David Rintoul stars

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.desc: The lawyer defends an MP accused of sexually assaulting one of his assistants, but finds the accused politician reluctant to defend himself. Meanwhile, Rumpoles favorite clerk is accused of theft. Courtroom drama, guest starring Anton Rodgers, with Leo McKern

programme.desc: A 16th-century Scottish clansman discovers he is one of a race of immortal warriors who can only be killed by beheading, and who are destined to fight to the death until just one survives. programme.title: Little House on the Prairie

Most of Walnut Grove is happy to welcome Blanche into their community, but the trouble starts when Harriet Oleson gets involved. programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Isaiah makes a promise to a dying man that he will find a home for his little girl, only to realise the infant is a baby orang-utan which wreaks havoc in Walnut Grove. programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: A hailstorm leaves the Ingalls familys crops in ruin. To recoup the money, Charles and some friendly neighbours - including a light-hearted Englishman - work in a dangerous railroad blasting job. programme.title: Little House on the Prairie

programme.title: The Ruth Rendell Mysteries

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.desc: Part one of two. Tom and Deborah head off for a well-earned holiday, but their hopes of getting away from it all are dashed when they realise that Len and Linda are staying at a campsite nearby. Back in Ormston, Arthur is unexpectedly called away from his duties in the village, and the impending hospital visit by fiery local councillor Eugenia Maddox (guest star Frances de la Tour) prompts the residents to rally round to make a good impression

programme.title: Doctor Finlay
programme.desc: Most TV characters are incapable of straying very far from their native haunts without either finding romance or stumbling over a murder and Dr Finlay is no exception. Here, his much-needed Highland walking holiday ends in the former, but it still turns out to be pretty hectic. David Rintoul stars

programme.title: The Antiques Riviera
programme.desc: Foluke is downsizing her home, but parting with her 200 beloved antique boxes is proving a big problem. She needs a buyer bold enough to take the lot. For 86-year-old Ben, a Spitfire flight is the dream of a lifetime - but first he needs to turn his vintage audio kit into £3000 of hard cash. A 700-piece Barbie collection could hold the key to funding urgent house repairs

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: New staff nurse Sophie attends to 22-year-old chef James, who accidentally stabbed himself in the leg while chopping tomatoes. During an eventful shift, she also uses chest compressions to keep alive another patient and helps 54-year-old Bernard, who was out cold for 10 minutes after falling and banging his head against a car. However, when his family arrives, it turns out he had been drinking

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Consultant Jacqui must act fast when severely underweight baby Jaziah is rushed into resus with breathing difficulties. In paediatric AandE, it is hoped that 18-month-old Charlies urine test results will explain why he has been feeling unwell for five days, while 81-year-old Ronald prepares for a CT scan after suffering a suspected stroke that is affecting his speech

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of favourite investigations from the team. Jimmy Doherty hears about a threat to British juniper berries that could mark the end of gins recent resurgence in popularity, while Kate Quilton delves into the contents of herbal bedtime teas to establish if they really can help people get to sleep more easily. Big oyster fan Matt Tebbutt wonders why he never finds pearls in his seafood platters so sets off for China, where he discovers that pearl production is full of surprises

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The weeks winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon OBrien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops around the Charente region of western France to find house-hunters Debbie and Guy their perfect forever home where they can start up their own gîte business. With a maximum budget of £135,000, will any of the five properties Jasmine shows them hit the mark?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in the north east is 45-year-old clothes shop owner Lee, who is hoping to impress her guests with an evening dedicated to her favourite kind of food. She kicks off with a Greek salad, although its unlike most Greek salads that her guests - hairdresser Simon, nurse Sophie, sexual health nurse Jade and emergency planner James - have ever had

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Simon hopes that tried-and-tested nutritious food will impress his guests on the second night in the North East. But not everyone is convinced as Lee has issues with the dessert, while Sophie enjoys grilling the host about the menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Sophie hosts the third night in the North East, hoping her fast-food menu and kids party theme will let her guests unleash their inner children and win her the prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night in the north-east, sexual health nurse Jade throws a Wild West-themed party in a bid to rope her the money. James channels his inner cowboy, complete with authentic-ish American accent, and after some flattering food chat, the gang wonders whether hes on a charm offensive because his night is still to come. After Jade and Simons earlier tiff, everyone watches in anticipation to see whether he will pick her meal apart. With the party in full flow, Lee is shocked to find out Jades job, but the others arent happy with her behaviour around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in the north-east, emergency planner James hopes to impress the group with a carefully constructed menu. It being their final meeting, the participants open up and Jade is surprised to learn that Lee had a negative first impression of her. Sophie comes clean about her previous views on host James, which leaves the group members flabbergasted

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Grange Farm Park in Maltby le Marsh, Lincolnshire, which is run by mum and daughter team Ann Graves and Rebecca OBrien. Guests Emily and Warren marvel at their luxury room, while father and son Bis and Deep cant wait to dive into their hot tub. But Marilyn and Neil are concerned that their bathroom might be a bit cramped. After a day out feeding baby seals at a local seal sanctuary, the BandBers congregate at the on-site restaurant for dinner, where Marilyn isnt pleased to find out that she and Neil are the only guests without a hot tub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Marilyn and Neil Tuson host the second night of the week at Edgecliff Boutique BandB in Birchington-on-Sea, Kent - hoping the breathtaking views will give them the edge they need. Guests Emily and Warren are big fans of their rooms sea views, but Bis and Deep find a dirty kettle and Ann and Rebecca arent happy about a dusty chandelier. Later, in the morning, theres a long wait at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Veras Kitchen and BandB in Lechlade, Gloucestershire, where young owners Warren Neinaber and Emily Mapson hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests check in to the Preston Hotel in Yeovil, owned by father and son Bis and Deep Oozageer. Ann and Rebecca find their room more cheap than cheerful, although an afternoon tasting the produce at a cider brandy distillery proves a massive hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners reunite, ready to air their grievances from throughout the week. Ann and Rebecca from Grange Farm Park arent happy with Marilyn marking them down for the lack of a hot tub, and Marilyn herself isnt too pleased when her and Neil review the feedback for their slow breakfast service at Edgecliff Boutique BandB

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Citroën SM - widely considered one of the greatest French cars ever - after it suffered catastrophic engine failure on its first outing

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head to Norfolk to take on a 1962 TVR Grantura that has been off the road for nearly 50 years

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Pomeranian Prince is ready to rule his kingdom, but it remains to be seen if the Acton family will be his subjects. Winnies obsession with chewing toys could be his downfall

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Back in 2011, Edward and Hazel Short set out to build an art deco, shining white lighthouse tower with additional bedrooms, guest rooms and infinity pool, all on a rugged but beautiful clifftop in north Devon. It was an ambitious project and quickly ran into problems, not least of which were the apparent breakdown of the couples marriage and a spiral of borrowing and debt, leaving Edward facing bankruptcy. Kevin McCloud now returns to see if anything has been salvaged from this disastrous project

programme.title: Astrid: Murder in Paris
programme.desc: A famous actor is shot on a film set during an exchange of supposedly blank gunfire. Was it an accident or a carefully planned murder? Astrid and Raphaëlle discover that he was having an affair with the directors wife, but a series of clues suddenly puts them on the trail of a criminal they crossed swords with a few years earlier. In Frnech

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of three mothers who have triumphed over extraordinary adversity. One was diagnosed with cervical cancer when she was 19, one has suffered the death of a child after he fell from scaffolding working as a bricklayer, and another faced the challenge of bringing up a child who needed extra care, and who has suffered potential injuries to his pelvis having been thrown off his motorbike at speed

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: An 18-year-old is rushed in after suffering a cardiac arrest during a football game and 30-year-old Tom has a severe ankle injury after a head-on traffic collision. In the Minor Injuries department 23-year-old Phoebe has a damaged nose after tripping in the library

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: In Worcester, paramedics are called to reports of a man unconscious in the town centre. A friend says hes been taking drugs, so they quickly get him into the ambulance. A 27-stone man has lost the vision in one eye and has very high blood pressure. Getting him out of the house and into an ambulance requires specialist help and equipment

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of three mothers who have triumphed over extraordinary adversity. One was diagnosed with cervical cancer when she was 19, one has suffered the death of a child after he fell from scaffolding working as a bricklayer, and another faced the challenge of bringing up a child who needed extra care, and who has suffered potential injuries to his pelvis having been thrown off his motorbike at speed

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: An 18-year-old is rushed in after suffering a cardiac arrest during a football game and 30-year-old Tom has a severe ankle injury after a head-on traffic collision. In the Minor Injuries department 23-year-old Phoebe has a damaged nose after tripping in the library

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team revisits favourite investigations, including Kate Quiltons visit to the worlds largest producer of jelly beans to find out what goes into the sweets. Matt Tebbutt attempts to find out why tinned anchovies taste so salty, and Jimmy Doherty heads to a Mauritius distillery as he explores the secrets of rum

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sailing fans Nicole and Lee are planning to move permanently to Aude in the south of France. With three children between them from previous relationships, theyre looking for a property with plenty of space for visitors. Jean Johansson shows the couple five properties for their maximum budget of £270,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Fiona and David find their perfect holiday home in Cyprus on a budget of £100,000. As a former Royal Navy engineer who used to be based in Cyprus, David knows the island well, but Jasmine hopes she can find them the ideal spot

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Greg and Tina are searching for a holiday home in western Cyprus, which they plan to share with all their family. Greg has been visiting Cyprus for nearly 40 years, and after introducing Tina to the island, she has fallen in love with it too. Helping them to find the perfect place is property expert Leah Charles-King, who shows them the very best of what their £190,000 budget can buy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Castle Arms Inn in Snape, North Yorkshire, where owner Jill Chinnery hopes the charms of her 17th-century coaching inn will give her the edge

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers enjoy the great outdoors at Eden Valley Glamping in Cumbria, a site with a back-to-basics approach - but a dusty bathroom and damp duvets put a crimp in the guests stay

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Fernie Castle in Letham, Fife, where owners Neil and Mary Blackburn hope to impress their guests with their traditional 15th century BandB

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests check in to the Cadillac Kustomz Hotel on the Isle of Bute, where the retro design proves popular but the Scottish-American breakfast seems to get lost in translation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners meet up for a final showdown, as Jill from the Castle Arms Inn takes issue with her feedback - wanting to quiz Neil and Mary on their curtain comments in particular. Next, Liz and Dave hope the others will eat their words over the breakfast complaints they found hard to swallow at their Eden Valley Glamping site in Cumbria

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Receptionist Linzey hosts the first dinner party in Durham, where she hopes to woo her visitors with an evening of flirty fun and a date-night menu of food she loves to eat. Her guests are 48-year-old teacher Rob, 44-year-old events planner Kate, 25-year-old beach enthusiast Elliot and 33-year-old health worker Kenya

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teacher Rob takes charge in Durham, hoping to get top marks with a range of dishes based on different school lessons. But theres a surprise for the group when a new face turns up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Third to host in Durham is events planner and staunch vegan Kate, who is hoping to win with a Roaring Twenties theme and a strictly vegan menu. But will the meat eaters be left wanting or will Kates party planning skills see her schmooze her way to the top of the table?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Durham, the fourth host is recruitment consultant and ex-trampolining pro Matthew, who hopes to impress his guests with an Ibiza-themed party, where Spanish food and full-on clubbing are on the menu. It remains to be seen whether Matthew - or Matty to his friends - finds his food fiesta and spot of impromptu flips and stretches successfully put his guests in the mood

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The final host in Durham is 33-year-old health worker and big-time party girl Kenya, whos hoping to impress her guests with an East African menu, complete with safari theme. Kenyas wild nature has provided many memorable moments throughout the week, and shes looking to take that energy into the kitchen to put on an evening to remember

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Star and Garter in Linlithgow, West Lothian, where guests have connectivity concerns as well as worries over the water supply in the morning

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals spend the night at Larch Green Lodges in the Renfrewshire village of Langbank on the banks of the River Clyde. The guests are wowed by the sweeping views of the estuary but cleanliness concerns and a black pudding blunder at breakfast lead to negative comments come feedback time

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals stay at the Distant Hills Guest House in the Scottish Highlands, where the scenic views prove popular and an afternoon of quad-biking send spirits soaring

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rival hoteliers final visit of the week is to Edinburgh bed-and-breakfast establishment 18 Craigmillar Park, run by Craig Bonnar. From the outset, the guests are impressed by ex-paratrooper Craigs in-room military precision. During the in-house whisky tasting experience, Craig reveals his passion for a dram. At breakfast, the hot stuff goes down handsomely, but the plastic packaged continental causes concern

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final day of the competition sees the hosts meet for the last time, to find out what theyve been paid and to settle some scores. Shona and John of Larch Green Lodges are determined to flush out Dani and Camerons dirty remarks. Moving on to Distant Hills Guest House, Dani and Cameron want to get to the bottom of Craigs water woes, while Craig himself wants to clean up a filthy finding at his BandB 18 Craigmillar Park

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Stuart, whos 40, is admitted to hospital after being involved in a high-speed collision with multiple cyclists during a race, and a CT scan reveals that he is bleeding internally. programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: New staff nurse Sophie attends to 22-year-old chef James, who accidentally stabbed himself in the leg while chopping tomatoes. During an eventful shift, she also uses chest compressions to keep alive another patient and helps 54-year-old Bernard, who was out cold for 10 minutes after falling and banging his head against a car. However, when his family arrives, it turns out he had been drinking

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Consultant Jacqui must act fast when severely underweight baby Jaziah is rushed into resus with breathing difficulties. In paediatric AandE, it is hoped that 18-month-old Charlies urine test results will explain why he has been feeling unwell for five days, while 81-year-old Ronald prepares for a CT scan after suffering a suspected stroke that is affecting his speech

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: When a car collides with two children walking home from school, two ambulances, a doctor and a critical care paramedic are sent to the scene. Paramedics also treat a man whos having back-to-back epileptic seizures, and a four-month-old baby with a barking cough is finding it increasingly difficult to breathe

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called to a rare and unusual stomach condition thats left a patient in agony. On the night shift, a young man is intoxicated and passed out in the street. An elderly man has fallen at home and cant get back up, and a confused and disorientated woman has one of the highest blood pressure readings the paramedics have ever seen

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second host of the week is football fan Adam in Bristol who dedicates his dinner to his favourite sport, unfortunately his guests dont share his enthusiasm. Tensions at the table rise between Jade and Sarah leading to a red card from the host

programme.title: The Antiques Riviera
programme.desc: Foluke is downsizing her home, but parting with her 200 beloved antique boxes is proving a big problem. She needs a buyer bold enough to take the lot. For 86-year-old Ben, a Spitfire flight is the dream of a lifetime - but first he needs to turn his vintage audio kit into £3000 of hard cash. A 700-piece Barbie collection could hold the key to funding urgent house repairs

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Another selection of favourite investigations from the team. Jimmy Doherty hears about a threat to British juniper berries that could mark the end of gins recent resurgence in popularity, while Kate Quilton delves into the contents of herbal bedtime teas to establish if they really can help people get to sleep more easily. Big oyster fan Matt Tebbutt wonders why he never finds pearls in his seafood platters so sets off for China, where he discovers that pearl production is full of surprises

programme.title: The Perfect Pitch
programme.desc: Ending their tour of north Wales in search of the perfect pitch, the seasoned campers arrive at The Oyster Farm Caravan Site on Anglesey. Marine biologist-turned-campsite host Shaun welcomes the campers with freshly caught oysters and his own oyster gin, before sending them on an aquatic tour of the nearby Puffin Island. The weeks winner of the coveted plaque and £2,000 cash prize is then announced

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon OBrien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Property expert Jasmine Harman pulls out all the stops around the Charente region of western France to find house-hunters Debbie and Guy their perfect forever home where they can start up their own gîte business. With a maximum budget of £135,000, will any of the five properties Jasmine shows them hit the mark?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in the north east is 45-year-old clothes shop owner Lee, who is hoping to impress her guests with an evening dedicated to her favourite kind of food. She kicks off with a Greek salad, although its unlike most Greek salads that her guests - hairdresser Simon, nurse Sophie, sexual health nurse Jade and emergency planner James - have ever had

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Simon hopes that tried-and-tested nutritious food will impress his guests on the second night in the North East. But not everyone is convinced as Lee has issues with the dessert, while Sophie enjoys grilling the host about the menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nurse Sophie hosts the third night in the North East, hoping her fast-food menu and kids party theme will let her guests unleash their inner children and win her the prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the fourth night in the north-east, sexual health nurse Jade throws a Wild West-themed party in a bid to rope her the money. James channels his inner cowboy, complete with authentic-ish American accent, and after some flattering food chat, the gang wonders whether hes on a charm offensive because his night is still to come. After Jade and Simons earlier tiff, everyone watches in anticipation to see whether he will pick her meal apart. With the party in full flow, Lee is shocked to find out Jades job, but the others arent happy with her behaviour around the table

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in the north-east, emergency planner James hopes to impress the group with a carefully constructed menu. It being their final meeting, the participants open up and Jade is surprised to learn that Lee had a negative first impression of her. Sophie comes clean about her previous views on host James, which leaves the group members flabbergasted

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Grange Farm Park in Maltby le Marsh, Lincolnshire, which is run by mum and daughter team Ann Graves and Rebecca OBrien. Guests Emily and Warren marvel at their luxury room, while father and son Bis and Deep cant wait to dive into their hot tub. But Marilyn and Neil are concerned that their bathroom might be a bit cramped. After a day out feeding baby seals at a local seal sanctuary, the BandBers congregate at the on-site restaurant for dinner, where Marilyn isnt pleased to find out that she and Neil are the only guests without a hot tub

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Marilyn and Neil Tuson host the second night of the week at Edgecliff Boutique BandB in Birchington-on-Sea, Kent - hoping the breathtaking views will give them the edge they need. Guests Emily and Warren are big fans of their rooms sea views, but Bis and Deep find a dirty kettle and Ann and Rebecca arent happy about a dusty chandelier. Later, in the morning, theres a long wait at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at Veras Kitchen and BandB in Lechlade, Gloucestershire, where young owners Warren Neinaber and Emily Mapson hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests check in to the Preston Hotel in Yeovil, owned by father and son Bis and Deep Oozageer. Ann and Rebecca find their room more cheap than cheerful, although an afternoon tasting the produce at a cider brandy distillery proves a massive hit

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandB owners reunite, ready to air their grievances from throughout the week. Ann and Rebecca from Grange Farm Park arent happy with Marilyn marking them down for the lack of a hot tub, and Marilyn herself isnt too pleased when her and Neil review the feedback for their slow breakfast service at Edgecliff Boutique BandB

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Citroën SM - widely considered one of the greatest French cars ever - after it suffered catastrophic engine failure on its first outing

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw head to Norfolk to take on a 1962 TVR Grantura that has been off the road for nearly 50 years

programme.title: The Dog House Australia
programme.desc: Pomeranian Prince is ready to rule his kingdom, but it remains to be seen if the Acton family will be his subjects. Winnies obsession with chewing toys could be his downfall

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Back in 2011, Edward and Hazel Short set out to build an art deco, shining white lighthouse tower with additional bedrooms, guest rooms and infinity pool, all on a rugged but beautiful clifftop in north Devon. It was an ambitious project and quickly ran into problems, not least of which were the apparent breakdown of the couples marriage and a spiral of borrowing and debt, leaving Edward facing bankruptcy. Kevin McCloud now returns to see if anything has been salvaged from this disastrous project

programme.title: Astrid: Murder in Paris
programme.desc: A famous actor is shot on a film set during an exchange of supposedly blank gunfire. Was it an accident or a carefully planned murder? Astrid and Raphaëlle discover that he was having an affair with the directors wife, but a series of clues suddenly puts them on the trail of a criminal they crossed swords with a few years earlier. In Frnech

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The stories of three mothers who have triumphed over extraordinary adversity. programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.desc: The guests check in to the Cadillac Kustomz Hotel on the Isle of Bute, where the retro design proves popular but the Scottish-American breakfast seems to get lost in translation

programme.title: Weathered: Earths Extremes

programme.title: Egypts Darkest Hour
programme.desc: Part one of two. Over 4,000 years ago, the ancient kingdom of Egypt, collapsed and disappeared. The mighty pharaohs, who had reigned supreme for more than seven centuries, lost control and the land was plunged into 150 years of chaos and civil war. In this documentary, a team of archaeologists examines the Tomb of the Warriors, a rare mass grave dating to the fall of the Old Kingdom, when political infighting and a changing climate brought down an empire

programme.title: Egypts Darkest Hour
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Tomb of the Warriors, near Luxor in southern Egypt, contains over 60 mummified bodies, all of which had suffered violent deaths. Though discovered in 1923 before being sealed, the tomb has only recently been investigated for what it can reveal of this lost world

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Joann Fletcher looks at the lives of ordinary Egyptian citizens 3,500 years ago. In the first of two programmes, she explores the conditions of their tightly packed houses, how they dressed and what they ate. Joann also examines their love of poetry, their enthusiasm for interior design and what it was like to work in the Valley of the Kings, where the pharaohs were entombed

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Part two of two. Joann Fletcher investigates the Ancient Egyptian attitude to death, revealing that to them life was just a dress rehearsal for the perfect afterlife that they were trying to reach. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines mummies to discover just why they spent a fortune preparing for death - and what they hoped to find when they got there

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: Archaeologist and historian Joann Fletcher explores the story of Ancient Egypt and begins with a search of the building blocks that made he civilisation so enduring. She discovers how its people changed from primitive farmers to pyramid builders in just a few centuries, and finds early evidence for Egypts gods and its obsession with death and the afterlife. She also reveals how the first writing was used to calculate taxes and examines Egypts first pyramid - one of the worlds earliest stone structures

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: The professor explores one of Saqqaras last pyramid complexes to illustrate how Ancient Egypts Pyramid Age came to an end. A worsening climate combined with political upheaval, famine and economic difficulties to plunge the state into a dark era of civil war, with the land dividing into smaller city-states headed by ambitious small-town leaders. In an obscure tomb in Thebes, she uncovers the stories of warriors who fought in the bloody battle that would eventually lead to Egypts reunification, and reveals how settlers known as the Hyksos tried to infiltrate the fledgling nations government to seize the throne

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: Joann explores the peak of ancient Egyptian civilisation by looking at the Colossi of Memnon, two massive stone statues depicting Pharaoh Amenhotep III, and examining the lives of the workers and artisans involved in the building of the Valley of the Kings. This golden age was threatened by the growing power of Karnaks priests, and Joann reveals how Tutankhamens early death was a chance for Egypt to start afresh and rewrite history, but was undermined by decades of state-sanctioned looting of the tombs

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: The historian reveals hows Egypts enemies exploited a country weakened by internal strife, travelling south to Sudan to examine the story of the forgotten Nubian kings, who ruled Egypt from their southern homeland for a century, even building their own pyramids to bury their monarchs. She then explores how the next group of invaders the Saites took the tradition of mummification to new extremes by preserving millions of animals, before Alexander the Great came to Egypts rescue and founded the greatest city on Earth

programme.title: Alexandria: The Greatest City
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes examines the history of the ancient city, which was founded by Alexander the Great in 331 BC. Until its decline in the fourth and fifth centuries AD, it became the centre of the world of art and culture and an example of advanced civilisation

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and his team of experts welcome James to the workshop who brings Dominic Chinea a 1960s lawnmower to repair. As a child James and his brother would visit their grandfather and use the machine to see who could mow the lawn the quickest before the whole family would play cricket on the grass. James thinks now is the right time to put his own son to work using his great grandfathers lawnmower. Brenton West gets a visit from Lyn who, as the secretary for the Welsh Schools Rugby Union Intermediate Group, brings him a trophy to bring back to its former glory. With almost one hundred silver plaques on dating back to 1904, the mahogany shield has a large crack in it and the decorative centre panel has lost its detail. Suzie Fletcher receives a visit from Deborah who asks her to repair her personalised leather belt that has held her jeans up for over 40 years. As a gift for her 16th birthday, the leather is now dry and splitting and needs a colour refresh

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott delight in Devonshire, where cream teas and antique treasures are on the menu. Amid the local ships, the pair dig up some wooden toys, prized pottery and very shiny things to sell. Irita visits an antiques emporium thats so new, it hasnt even opened yet and also gets her hands on something that brings back happy memories of childhood. Raj, meanwhile, heads off to hear the story of one of the great adventurers of the 20th century, as told by the son of the great man himself

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott embrace their inner pirate as they plunder the shops of the Cornish peninsula. Raj makes some big finds in one of the tiniest shops of the trip and pins his hopes on a risky item that may or may not be highly collectable. Irita plumps for a very cheap purchase and finds a painting that reminds her of her childhood and also contains a hidden secret. Along the way theres still time for some sightseeing, kite-flying and, of course, pirate impressions. The pair then head to the final auction to see who will emerge victorious

programme.desc: The barrister examines the cases of Arash Ghorbani-Zarin, who was stabbed to death by his girlfriends brothers. programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines the case of Anthony Richardson, who was attacked in broad daylight outside a shopping centre in Grimsby. Plus, how teenager Carly Fairhursts decision to become a prison pen pal to convicted killer Darren Pilkington would change her own life and end in tragedy

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 1
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott hit the road again, winding each other up, cracking bad jokes and looking for exquisite antiques, beginning in north Wales. Raj also heads for a remote but stunning stretch of Welsh coastline to discover how the national language was brought close to extinction before becoming a defining feature of modern Wales

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 2
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott bid goodbye to Wales and say hello to Shropshire as their journey continues. programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 3
programme.desc: As they hit the halfway point in their trip, Irita Marriott and Raj Bisram meander through the Midlands where Iritas eye is caught by six silver goblets and Raj mistakes a silver candle snuffer for a Georgian toddy ladle

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 4
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott explore The Fens in search of treasure, ticking off five east of England counties in their trusty Vanden Plas Princess. As the competition gets down to the pointy end, they apply their business heads on but still cannot resist some shenanigans on the way. At the shops, Raj falls in love with the oldest thing hes ever found on the Road Trip, even if he cant pronounce whats written on the ticket. Irita discovers a secret door barred to the general public and discovers long-forgotten and very dusty treasures

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 5
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott head to the seaside in Lincolnshire, for their last chance to hit the shops before the final auction showdown. At the shops, Irita comes over all nostalgic as she stumbles across items from her childhood. She also gets her hands on some exquisite, enamelled artwork that she feels would benefit from being dismantled. Raj racks up a haul of potential purchases, but still finds time for a spot of puppetry. He also discovers the birthplace of the package holiday and meets the towns jovial mascot who has been pulling in the punters for over 100 years

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 1
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott hit the road again, winding each other up, cracking bad jokes and looking for exquisite antiques, beginning in north Wales. Raj also heads for a remote but stunning stretch of Welsh coastline to discover how the national language was brought close to extinction before becoming a defining feature of modern Wales

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 2
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott bid goodbye to Wales and say hello to Shropshire as their journey continues. Irita has her eye on a number of items, each one bigger than the last while Raj sets his sights on some coloured glass and rediscovers the object that kicked off his career in the antiques game

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 3
programme.desc: As they hit the halfway point in their trip, Irita Marriott and Raj Bisram meander through the Midlands where Iritas eye is caught by six silver goblets and Raj mistakes a silver candle snuffer for a Georgian toddy ladle

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 4
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott explore The Fens in search of treasure, ticking off five east of England counties in their trusty Vanden Plas Princess. As the competition gets down to the pointy end, they apply their business heads on but still cannot resist some shenanigans on the way. At the shops, Raj falls in love with the oldest thing hes ever found on the Road Trip, even if he cant pronounce whats written on the ticket. Irita discovers a secret door barred to the general public and discovers long-forgotten and very dusty treasures

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.sub-title: Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott - Day 5
programme.desc: Experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott head to the seaside in Lincolnshire, for their last chance to hit the shops before the final auction showdown. At the shops, Irita comes over all nostalgic as she stumbles across items from her childhood. She also gets her hands on some exquisite, enamelled artwork that she feels would benefit from being dismantled. Raj racks up a haul of potential purchases, but still finds time for a spot of puppetry. He also discovers the birthplace of the package holiday and meets the towns jovial mascot who has been pulling in the punters for over 100 years

programme.desc: Six chefs become four as finals week begins. programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Sidney takes Geordie to Johnny Johnsons London jazz club, hoping to leave their worries in Grantchester for the night, but the backstage murder of Johnnys sister Claudette puts paid to that. When Archie, the victims father and owner of the club, calls in a police officer pal to crack the case, Geordie immediately detects the copper is corrupt, while Sidney makes an important discovery about Claudette that she had kept hidden from all but those closest to her. As every lead seems to come back to Archies violent past, recriminations within the family begin to play out

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: When Foxs jilted boyfriend threatens to beat him up, Mark asks Pop to teach him to fight

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire Rick meets inventor James Dyson, who allows him special access to his futuristic farm, where robots tend to strawberries all year round. Chef Rachel Green cooks Rick traditional plum bread, he also meets pig farmers producing Lincolnshire sausages. He then cooks strawberry sorbet and bangers and mash

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Amid Belfasts buzzy food scene, Rick tucks into Filipino-style Sunday lunch. On Northern Irelands Atlantic coast, he meets an award-winning chef, with a no waste approach, who turns fish offal and under-used species such as dog fish into stunning dishes. Rick also makes his first Filipino dish, and, after witnessing the dry-ageing of some special Irish beef, cooks beef and Guinness pie

programme.title: Eva Longoria: Searching For
programme.desc: Eva explores Madrids vibrant food scene, finds out why the city is being dubbed the New Miami and joins the celebrations at the citys gay pride parade

programme.title: The Report (2019)
programme.desc: A senator recruits an idealistic member of her staff to lead an investigation of the CIAs Detention and Interrogation Program created in the aftermath of the September 11 attacks. He is shocked to uncover efforts made in the intelligence service to destroy evidence, subvert the law, and hide a shocking secret from the American public. Fact-based drama, with Adam Driver, Corey Stoll, Annette Bening and Jon Hamm

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Annie MacManus, Huw Stephens and MayKay present another selection of live performances and interviews from Dingle and west Wales, this week featuring Chalk, Miles Kane, Annie-Dog and Travy

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Babatunde Aleshe introduces Chloe Petts and Lindsey Santoro to Londons Hammersmith Apollo stage, where they perform their stand-up routines

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Levi talks some sense into Remi, Lacey opens up to Holden and Cash is forced to lie to his wife

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Cash doubles down on his lie. Sonny snaps at Dana. Keri resorts to some of her old tricks

programme.title: The Wish Swap (2025)
programme.desc: Two strangers celebrating their birthdays in the same restaurant each make a wish at the same time - but each ends up having the others wish granted. Romantic fantasy, starring Emily Tennant and Jake Foy

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The quarter-final sees the five remaining sewers make garments inspired by divas past and present. For the Pattern Challenge, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young take Tina Turners iconic fringed dress as inspiration and challenge the sewers to create their own versions with multiple layers of fringing. The Transformation Challenge envisages the diva on their day off, still fabulous but this time slipping into something more comfortable in animal print. Finally, in the Made to Measure, the sewers get to choose a stage outfit for their favourite diva. Hosted by Kiell Smith-Bynoe

programme.title: Animal Control
programme.desc: Emily and Victoria help Dr Summers with an alpaca birth, while Frank visit his dad Jimmy with Patel

programme.title: UCL Magazine Show
programme.desc: Including reviews of recent Champions League matches, a round-up of the latest news, plus interviews with leading players and coaches

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v Italy (Kick-off 4.40pm). Jacqui Hurley is joined by Jamie Heaslip, Simon Zebo, and Stephen Ferris to present coverage of both teams fifth and final match of the championship, held at Principality Stadium. Wales were beaten 22-15 by Italy when the teams met at Stadio Olimpico in last years competition and are once again without a win in this seasons championship, having last won a Six Nations Contest in 2023. They will face an Italian side brimming with confidence after a historic 23-18 home win against England last time out. With commentary by Des Curran and Darren Cave

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: France v England (Kick-off 8.10pm). Jacqui Hurley is joined by Jamie Heaslip, Simon Zebo, and Stephen Ferris to present all the action from the final match of the championship, held at Stade de France. England claimed a narrow 26-25 victory over France at Allianz Stadium Twickenham in last years competition, but it was Les Bleus who went on to win the title. Frances Grand Slam hopes were dashed by Scotland last time out, but they are still very much in contention to retain the championship, while England have endured a disappointing campaign, winning just one of their four games. With commentary by Hugh Cahill and Bernard Jackma

programme.title: Beast (2022)
programme.desc: Recently widowed Nate Samuels and his teenage daughters Meredith and Norah find their holiday at a South African nature reserve turns into a nightmare when they are hunted by a huge rogue lion in the savanna.
