programme.desc: Robbie Cumming travels along the Rochdale Canal from Sowerby Bridge to Manchester. But disaster strikes when he runs aground, threatening to stop him from reaching his final destination

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital has its hands full when a typhoid epidemic breaks out in the community. Things get worse as the diseases source is traced back to the wards and staff struggle to locate it. Dr Weatherill is forced to reprimand Dr Ormerod after catching him sleeping on the job - but also has startling news for him. Drama, starring Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons in Essex try to win the affections of podium dancer Ciara by picking an outfit of their choice for her to wear

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In London, three singletons go up against each other in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of self-proclaimed princess Winter. To impress her, they have to dress her, but Winter has no information to go on but the outfits bought. Which of the three competitors will she choose for a prized first date?

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The second and concluding part of an interview with the former Republic of Ireland star, as he discusses with Tommy Martin the high esteem he was held in for club and country while facing battles on and off the pitch

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Griff Rhys Jones, Catherine Tyldesley, Denise Van Outen and AJ Odudu take to the kitchen in a series of head to head cooking battles. They each attempt to create the perfect Italian dish, and avoid elimination in the Cook Off. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Daniel Galmiche and Ruby Bhogal at the Wimpole Estate in Cambridgeshire to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day this time. Ainsley digs up some spuds for his quick flatbread pizza with Charlotte potatoes, rosemary and chilli rings, Daniel brings his French fervour to a pork chop with courgette, tomatoes and cavolo nero, and Ruby celebrates her Indian heritage with her mothers Indian spiced lamb kebab rolls with onion, mint, chilli and coriande

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2012

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news documentary looking back at the headline stories of the past 20 years, focusing this week on 2013

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The fossil record shows that the arapaima fish has roamed the waters of the Amazon River since the Jurassic age, and uses its bony tongue to crush prey against the roof of its mouth before swallowing. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade encountered the species once before and came out second best, so this time he is taking no chances as he investigates every aspect of this huge creature

programme.title: Inside Sydney Airport
programme.desc: The imminent arrival of the Prime Minister of Singapore has security on high alert, while Duty Manager Mel is excited to escort his favourite pop star

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host the celebrity cookery show in which famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the contestants is paired with a chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. In the first episode, Johnny Vegas, Harry Judd, Shirley Ballas and Naughty Boy take part

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Wendy, Jack, Susheel and Neil pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Contestants from across the UK take on Jenny The Vixen Ryan, Anne The Governess Hegerty, Shaun The Dark Destroyer Wallace, Mark The Beast Labbett and Paul The Sinnerman Sinha, hoping to win big money. Bradley Walsh hosts

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity interviews, entertainment news, current affairs discussions, a look at sports competitions, health columns, cooking tips and tricks, and more.

programme.title: John and Lisas Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare a menu perfect for enjoying inside and out. Dishes featured include spiced beer can chicken with saffron potatoes, homemade crisps and dips, summer cocktails, gammon steak, celeriac remoulade and coconut and polenta cake with honey syrup

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates the documentary series revealing the incredible ways in which all life is impacted throughout the four seasons

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.sub-title: A Year on My Planet Earth
programme.desc: The stories of the scientists and conservationists who used pioneering techniques to uncover new behaviour, enabling the film-makers to producer this extraordinary series

programme.title: Alan Quinlan: Red Blooded
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alan Quinlan talks to Joe Molloy about his Ireland career. The back-row forward won 27 international caps during a nine-year period full of ups and downs. In the second part, he discusses becoming only the second player to reach 200 appearances for Munster and how he dealt with anxiety and mental health throughout his rugby career

programme.title: Live: Guinness Six Nations
programme.desc: Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 2.10pm). All the action from the opening match in the fifth and final round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. Ireland have won the last 11 meetings between the teams, including a 32-18 triumph at Scottish Gas Murrayfield Stadium in last years competition and a narrow 17-13 victory here in 2024, but the Scots go into this level on points with France at the top of the table following their win against the French last time out. The Irish could also still win the title, but they need to win today and hope France are beaten by England

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the Gaelic football legend who grew up in The Troubles in Armagh, and recovered from a missed penalty to score the winning goal in their only All-Ireland win

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Archive news stories, looking back at the headlines from 1998

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: A look back at the stories making the headlines in 1999, set to a soundtrack of music from the year

programme.title: UEFA Man in the Middle
programme.desc: Set during the Group Stage of the 2019/20 UEFA Champions League season, episode two takes a deeper dive into the psychology of refereeing

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: Murder in the Pacific
programme.desc: The documentary continues with the investigation into the sinking of the Rainbow Warrior in May 1985. Just one day after the explosions that sunk the Greenpeace ship, police were overwhelmed with calls from people claiming to have spotted unusual goings on. One witness led to Swiss tourists Alain and Sophie Turenge. They came into the station voluntarily but their inability to answer simple questions led police to doubt their identities

programme.title: Million Dollar Listing: NYC
programme.desc: In Brooklyn, Ryan hustles to land his first new development under Serhant, and Fredrik celebrates his decision to go bi-coastal and announces new plans to expand his empire. Dreaming of a bigger team, KJ makes her own announcement, Steve hits the ice to lock down his 57th Street rental, and Tyler celebrates an emotional closing. After a wild year, the agents toast to new beginnings and the return to a new normal.

programme.title: Below Deck: Mediterranean
programme.desc: In a hectic final day, the crew tries to pull off a picnic in an old Croatian fortress, while Mathew struggles to pull himself out of a spiral

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Katie and Schwartz attempt to maintain a friendship in the wake of their recent divorce, and tensions between Schwartz and Lala bubble over

programme.title: British Grandma on Death Row with Susanna Reid
programme.desc: The Good Morning Britain host travels to Texas to meet 66-year-old British citizen Linda Carty, who was sentenced to death in 2002 for the murder of young mother Joanna Rodriguez so she could steal her newborn son. Carty has been awaiting execution for more than 20 years and Susanna hears her story and interviews lawyers on both sides of this case

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the Olympic boxing silver medallist, who discusses his journey to the top of the amateur boxing world and his hidden struggles with addiction

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host this quiz contest, in which five contestants race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try and win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Paul OGradys Animal Orphans
programme.desc: The comedian begins another tour of Zambia, Namibia and South Africa helping to hand-rear orphaned animals and prepare them for release back into their natural habitats. His first stop is the Moholoholo Wildlife Rehabilitation Centre, where he meets an eight-month-old rhino which lost its mother when she was killed by poachers. Next, he teaches a young aardvark how to hunt for termites, before providing some much-needed company for a lion cub which was raised as a pet until she got too big to handle

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: A sinister inquiry into skeletal remains found in a Brighton storm drain takes Grace down a labyrinthine trail through the past, but its not only on the professional front that he is chasing ghosts.

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity interviews, entertainment news, current affairs discussions, a look at sports competitions, health columns, cooking tips and tricks, and more.

programme.title: News at 12:30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team, followed by sport and weather

programme.title: Live Racing
programme.desc: Coverage of the fourth and final day of Cheltenham Festival, where the feature race is the 4.00 Boodles Cheltenham Gold Cup, and races also take place at 1.20, 2.00, 2.40, 3.20 and 4.40. Analysis comes from AP McCoy, Ruby Walsh, Mick Fitzgerald and Megan Nicholls, with reports by Matt Chapman, Alice Plunkett, Rishi Persad and Luke Harvey, Social Stable updates from Oli Bell, betting news with Brian Gleeson, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: A lively mix of celebrity chat with national and international guests, fabulous food from the country?s top Chefs, expert advice and all the unmissable stories making the headlines

programme.title: News at 7
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Princess Leia Fantasy
programme.desc: Ross foolishly lets Rachel in on his most secret sexual fantasy, while Phoebe consoles a heartbroken Monica and Chandler gets involved with an old flame whom Joey cannot stand. David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston star

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy receives a bit of encouraging news, while Gabby gives Arthur a fresh strategy to try out

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggie frets over Bens hospital appointment, a defamatory article upsets George, Alya meets with her former boss, and Hope urges Sam to stand up to his bully

programme.title: Betrayal
programme.desc: Veteran MI5 operative John Hughes is due to meet a British-Iranian informant who claims to have intelligence about a threat to national security, the man is killed before he can talk. All evidence points towards the death being a gangland dispute, but when John secretly meets with a source, the investigation brings him into dangerously close contact with a formidable Stockport gang. Spy thriller, starring Shaun Evans and Romola Garai

programme.title: The Day Diana Died
programme.desc: Piecing together the critical hours following the news of the Princess of Wales death and examining key decisions made by the Royal Household, newsrooms and Tony Blairs government. This programme looks at the moment the Queen was informed about Dianas death in Paris, and follows the return of her body to British soil. Featuring contributions from Tina Brown, Colin Tebbutt, Dickie Arbiter and Ian Hislop

programme.title: Gogglebox Ireland
programme.desc: Deirdre OKane provides the voiceover as a selection of telly addicts pass comment on the weeks programmes from the comfort of their living rooms

programme.title: Vaping Nation
programme.desc: One in every 12 people use vapes or e-cigarettes in Ireland, a figure that rises among young people. This documentary investigates the impact of vaping on users health, particularly among teenagers

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to Michelangelos near Newcastle, a 10-bedroom hotel, bar and Italian restaurant set in four acres. Back in 2019, just before the world changed due to Covid-19, Italian brothers Paolo and Giacomo called the Hotel Inspector. The pair had invested 20 years of hard work into the business, battled through Paulos cancer diagnosis and the death of their mother, and still owed almost half a million pounds. Did Alex help to turn their fortunes around?

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: As the team at the Hotel Benidorm Plaza prepare to throw a party to show off the hotel to local travel agents, the Spanish resort is hit by a rare rainstorm, resulting in the hotel springing leaks and causing the electricity to cut out. Meanwhile, a 14-strong friendship group party in memory of their dear friend, and a couple celebrate their 25th wedding anniversary at Benidorms famously romantic waterfalls

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: Millionaire Hot Seat
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson hosts the quiz where each episode sees six hopefuls compete for the £1 million prize - however, only one player can take their place in the Hot Seat at a time and answer multiple-choice questions to work their way up the Money Ladder. Unlike classic Millionaire, each question is against the clock and there is no room for indecision - only one player will have a chance to win the top prize remaining on the ladder, but will they be able to do so?

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian ends his stay at Battersea meeting the centres ugliest pooch - Bailey the shih-tzu - and American bulldog Krystal finds glitz and glamour with a walk-on part in Coronation Street. Meanwhile, lively Jack Russell Pepe has a pampered life to look forward to when he finds a posh new home

programme.title: Live: Guinness Six Nations
programme.desc: Ireland v Scotland (Kick-off 2.10pm). All the action from the opening match in the fifth and final round of fixtures, held at Aviva Stadium. Ireland have won the last 11 meetings between the teams, including a 32-18 triumph at Scottish Gas Murrayfield Stadium in last years competition and a narrow 17-13 victory here in 2024, but the Scots go into this level on points with France at the top of the table following their win against the French last time out. The Irish could also still win the title, but they need to win today and hope France are beaten by England

programme.title: Week in Review
programme.desc: Looking back at the main local, national and international stories of the week

programme.title: News at 5.30
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the Virgin Media News team

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts as Jane gets her chance to open the iconic red boxes - will she be swayed by one of the Bankers tempting offers for her randomly selected box, or will she hold out for a shot at the jackpot?

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, and meets those who just happened to be there at the right time to record jaw-dropping moments

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Ortis Deley, Maggie Aderin-Pocock, Graeme Hall and Sarah Millican pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: From Strictly to the sofa, Claudia kicks off her chat show which promises Hollywood actors, the best of British and comedy stars - all put at their ease by the hosts trademark warmth, charm and humour. Her first guests are Hollywood star and musician Jeff Goldblum, actress Vanessa Williams, comedy icon Jennifer Saunders and funnyman Tom Allen

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Another line-up of contestants answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines, including Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck. Jeremy Clarkson hosts

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi returns to Rosehill House, a 30-bedroom hotel in Burnley. When Alex first arrived two and a half years ago, she met a dad with a deadline and a daughter in despair. With the hotel badly underperforming, John was threatening to sell it and turn it into flats, but daughter Emma was unable to let it go. Have they managed to turn the business around?

programme.title: Coma
programme.desc: As Jordan remains in a coma, Simon is hailed a local hero, but when DS Evans begins investigating the assault she quickly spies holes in his story. Simons wife Beth starts to develop her own suspicions, while Jordans father Paul Franklin, a criminal with a history of extreme violence, starts digging for answers himself

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Journey to the Edge of the World
programme.desc: The comedian continues his journey through the remote regions of Canada, from the Atlantic to the Pacific by way of the Northwest Passage. Heading north to Baffin Island, Billy meets Inuit communities to learn about their lives and local trades and takes a two-day trek into the Auyuittuq National Park in the Arctic Circle, before talking to a tribal elder and joining one family on a seal hunt

programme.title: Cash Trapped
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Bradley Walsh in which six contestants battle it out across a series of fast-paced question rounds where the players must actively try to sabotage their opponents by beating them to the buzzer and trapping them out of the game. In the final round, the person who has earned the most cash will go head to head against the rest of the group to try to take their winnings home

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Ireland AM is the longest running daytime show in Ireland. Blending a mixture of lifestyle interviews and news content, with gardening, health, food and fashion seven days a week

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: The success of the Allied advance in Libya is undone when the Italian forces receive German reinforcements, leaving Stan and Rajib forced to beat a hasty retreat across the desert. Marga attends a maternity home and finds her commitment to the cause invigorated, despite an encounter with a traumatised fellow patient exposing her to some difficult truths. David is selected to fly a dangerous mission over France

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder follows the investigation into the suspicious death of 30-year-old Lyndsey Vaux, whose body was found in the Wigan home she shared with her partner Becky Reid and Beckys mum Gillian. Neighbours told police that the victim suffered regular abuse and beatings at the hands of her girlfriend. But when the pathology report proved inconclusive, police needed to find proof that she was responsible

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The Aidensfield policemen are called to subdue anti-war protestors at a disused airfield, but the event has serious repercussions for Walker after he accidentally hits one of the mob with his truncheon. Younger is gutted to discover Rosie has feelings for new vet Fergus, who is the indirect cause of chaos when a cobra escapes from his car at the garage. Irritated that nobodys taking her snake-catching suggestions seriously, Peggy refuses to help with the hunt and drives off with David - straight into the arms of danger. With Jonathan Kerrigan, Gwen Taylor, Vanessa Hehir and Andrew Langtree

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Martha fills in as the local Dear Debbie advice columnist, getting an anonymous letter that adds intrigue during a progressive dinner attended by all of our Middleton couples

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction and maintenance of the International Space Station, revealing the influence of an unconventional aircraft designer and a 19th-century rebreather

programme.title: Ainsleys National Trust Cook Off
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott is joined by chefs Ben Ebbrell and Cyrus Todiwala in the kitchen garden of Blickling Hall in Norfolk to see who can create and cook the Dish of the Day. Ainsley showcases stripey tomatoes picked from the Blickling greenhouse in his pan-fried mackerel, tomato and pomegranate salad, Ben brings his Latin American influences to the kitchen with his summer courgette chilaquiles with pico de gallo, and Cyrus creates an Indian taste sensation with his spicy chicken thighs and veg

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Hundreds died when the Sobral Santos II capsized not far from the shores of the Amazon in September 1981, and many of the stricken passengers were reportedly dragged to their deaths by powerful unseen attackers. Freshwater detective Jeremy Wade attempts to identify the culprits that played a part in the rivers worst-ever boating disaster, but has the trail gone cold after more than 30 years?

programme.title: Peter Andres 60 Minute Makeover
programme.desc: Peter Andres 60 Minute Makeover is back and bigger than ever on Quest Red. Peter and his army of helpers will be surprising deserving people across the country with a stunning home mkeover - performed in a day and brought to you in 60 minutes. In each episode one deserving person will get the surprise home makeover of a lifetime. Nominated by their friends and family they are everyday heroes with moving stories that tug at the heart.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village life is disrupted by a circus family who are accused of corrupting the vicars daughter, wrecking the shop and breaking into the church. Meanwhile, Nick and Kate are attacked for resisting local bigots. Eleanor Bron guest stars, with Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: A Year on Planet Earth
programme.desc: Stephen Fry narrates the documentary series revealing the incredible ways in which all life is impacted throughout the four seasons

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits a Blackpool hotelier who has grand plans for his seaside establishment, although a lack of star rating and a string of bad reviews have led to dwindling bookings. Having bought the then-derelict 15-bedroom property in 2009, the owner spent £250,000 trying to turn it into an upmarket destination more in keeping with a private members club

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham gives Rhona one last chance, while Arthur rejects Jais offer of a job and Nicola is suspicious

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jims old army mates join Steve at his funeral, Adams act of revenge puts Jodie on edge, Theo finds a new target, and Megans love life intrigues Leanne

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Dr Goodwin faces the pressure as rumours about him circulate the hospital and when Dr Weatherill passes on some important news, he wastes no time in using it to set the record straight. Nurse Taylors happiness about her future with Frankie is sensationally shattered as the extent of his dishonesty is revealed, while Matron is shocked to see a familiar face on the ward

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack finds himself working alongside DS Lawson on a delicate case involving a spate of attacks on defenceless senior citizens in their homes, while Lawson also offers him some advice on his personal life. David Jason stars, with Bruce Alexander, Sally Dexter and Neil Phillips

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick is seconded to a Scotland Yard team who have arrived in Aidensfield to investigate the fatal shooting of former policeman Andrew Gerard, and risks his career to ensure justice is done. Meanwhile, local man Alan Maskell is duped into helping a couple of crooks respray stolen cars. Nick Berry stars

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Kirsten uncovers evidence suggesting the murders were part of a coordinated attack. Her investigation takes her into the heart of Scottish politics, and the Naval cover up of a near catastrophe in the US. Amy starts to question who she can trust on Vigil, when a message from Kirsten makes Amy realise she is being deliberately undermined

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maggie frets over Bens hospital appointment, a defamatory article upsets George, Alya meets with her former boss, and Hope urges Sam to stand up to his bully

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy receives a bit of encouraging news, while Gabby gives Arthur a fresh strategy to try out

programme.title: Prime Suspect
programme.desc: Conclusion. Having seen her error of judgement cost a young boy his life, Tennison must use all her ingenuity to trap the man she believes is responsible - but firm evidence continues to elude her. Helen Mirren stars

programme.title: The Body in the Lake
programme.desc: Tim is disgusted with Dani and wants her gone. At the school, a suspended Dani gathers her artwork as Sienna tries to offer some comfort, and they discuss their shared experience of foster care. Bel asks a nervous Sienna to speak at the vigil for Zac that night. Tim is confronted by Dani about the texts, but he shrugs off her questions

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Vernon buys a batch of topsoil for his new compost-producing business, little suspecting it is from a contaminated landfill site. The shady dealer who sold him the soil winds up dead - as do a number of local cows - and Vernon is soon fighting for his life. Elsewhere, seductive sergeant Nokes sets her sights on Steve Crane, and Bellamy wonders whether Gina will return to Aidensfield and marry him. Geoffrey Hughes, James Carlton and Mark Jordon star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Bellamy chases joyriders tearing through Aidensfield and accidentally runs down Don Dibleys son during the pursuit. The boys father demands compensation, but soon realises his problems have just begun as his daughter reveals she is pregnant. Steve finds himself in trouble and David invents a novel way to rid the estate of moles. Rural drama, starring Mark Jordon, James Carlton and Ted Robbins

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A recently widowed woman arrives in Aidensfield wanting to bury her husband in the family grave, but the deceaseds brother refuses to co-operate because of an old feud. Vernon and David get involved and find themselves held at gunpoint as a shocking case of fraud is uncovered. Rural drama, starring Geoffrey Hughes and David Lonsdale

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a banks most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah sets Cain straight, while Liam urges Cain to inform Moira, who then struggles with the news

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain needs to face up to what his future might hold, while Joe feels unappreciated and Kim is surprised when she pays a visit to Moira

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rhona expresses her gratitude, but is spotted by Lydia, while Laurel confides in her friend Nicola

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Graham gives Rhona one last chance, while Arthur rejects Jais offer of a job and Nicola is suspicious

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy receives a bit of encouraging news, while Gabby gives Arthur a fresh strategy to try out

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: Paul meets a gentle giant Great Dane that is looking for a loving home, and a pair of bickering Lakeland Terriers who need to be taught how to reign in their mischievous behaviour. Elsewhere, he meets a feisty little Dachshund whose enormous bark makes up for his compact stature

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The discovery of a 2,000-year-old body provides fresh evidence in a five-year-old unsolved missing persons case, so Morse and Thursday take up the investigation, discovering a possible link to a local pagan ritual to celebrate the harvest. The investigation leads them to a nuclear power station, where they face a conspiracy of silence concerning an experimental new reactor and a potential disaster that could devastate Oxford. Guest starring Sheila Hancock, widow of original Inspector Morse star John Thaw

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help two people searching for their missing birth mothers - a woman taken to Morocco as toddler against her mothers will, and a man given up as a baby because of the colour of his skin. Nicky and Davina discover what happened - and that however difficult it may be, the truth has transformative power

programme.title: Titanic
programme.desc: Italian stoker Mario blags a job on the Titanic for his brother Paolo, who is instantly smitten with cabin steward Annie, and as the ship sets sail he startles her with an impulsive gesture. Barnes is surprised to find Watson loitering in steerage, while Mary lets her guard down with Peter Lubov, much to the anger of husband Jim. But any arguments are set aside when the ship strikes the iceberg - and fear quickly builds for the third-class passengers as some of them are behind locked gates

programme.title: Hotel Benidorm: Fun-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: New receptionist Ruben is about to experience his first unhappy customer as he works his first solo shift, while groom-to-be Dwain is whisked away from his stag do to get wedding-ready with fitness instructor Sarah. Also checking in today are two friends, with Michael treating his old pal JJ to a weekend away at their favourite holiday hotspot, and the pairs tickets arent the only thing he booked

programme.title: Alex Polizzi: My Hotel Nightmare
programme.desc: Alex has spent the last three months in lockdown in Cornwall with her family, overseeing the work at The Star from a distance. All work at the hotel had stopped temporarily, but its back up and running. When Alex is able to travel to Sussex to see the renovations, it becomes clear just how far behind they are. Because of delays and the possibility of now having a Christmas opening, Alex and Olga move the opening until Easter 2021

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: The detective and his team are called to the site of a fatal crash involving an articulated lorry, a car and a blue pick-up truck. An unexpected discovery found on the victim at the scene soon suggests there could be far more to this particular accident than meets the eye, sparking an unsettling chain of events that leads Grace and Branson on a game of cat and mouse through Brighton to save an innocent life before time runs out

programme.title: Outrage (1998)
programme.desc: A frustrated insurance agent and his wife report a trio of teenage vandals to the police. However, their act of good citizenship backfires disastrously when the crooks get released, putting them on the receiving end of a campaign of harassment - and forcing them to respond with extreme measures of their own. Drama, starring Rob Lowe and Jennifer Grey

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina Ward makes a surprise return to Aidensfield and Bellamy encounters a key informant who might come in useful - but finds himself in trouble when the man disappears before he can offer a tip-off about an imminent armed robbery. Meanwhile, Vernon acquires a bargain miniature railway which he hopes will impress the new estate manager, and it emerges Dr Liz Merrick may have feelings for Lord Ashfordlys recently appointed land agent. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon and Geoffrey Hughes star

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Dr Merrick faces a testing time when a terrified hospital patient claims her husband is trying to kill her, but Merton seems reluctant to get involved, while Vernon picks up a bargain all-purpose pest-control solution, enlisting Davids help in his eradication mission. Ginas cousin Diane arrives to help her and Phil with the wedding preparations - much to the delight of PC Crane, who is instantly smitten. Rural drama, starring Aislin McGuckin, Tricia Penrose and Mark Jordon

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Steve is forced to cover for Phil when the father-to-be discovers Gina has gone into labour two months early and rushes to the hospital to help with the nerve-racking delivery. Meanwhile, Merton is excited when it appears a drug dealer could soon be brought to justice. Drama, starring Mark Jordon, Tricia Penrose and Duncan Bell

programme.title: Agatha Christies Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirots help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: Vanderpump Rules
programme.desc: Theres a race to get Schwartz and Sandys Lounge ready for its first private event, and Raquel cosies up to SUR manager Peter to try to get over James

programme.title: Tell Me Everything
programme.desc: Louis wants to lose his virginity and Jonny gives him advice, but the tips backfire when the moment with Zia eventually comes. Jonny and Mei stage a lock-in at the pub, and Neve realises that she has feelings for Jonny

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A team of engineers are overhauling Londons underground infrastructure to build a world-class sewer system, and using cutting-edge tech and the latest construction methods, they work to transform a centuries-old network into a modern-day masterpiece

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Wendy, Jack, Susheel and Neil pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: The Chase Australia
programme.desc: Andrew OKeefe presents the Down Under version of the quiz show in which four people must work together to beat the Chaser and win a cash prize

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Billie, Seb, Victoria and Joshua vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Men from Manchester, Liverpool and Doncaster compete for the affections of property developer Chelsea

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: On the tropical island of Sulawesi, Indonesia, a tourist snorkeller and several local fishermen have been injured and killed by a sea creature that impales its victims. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to the scene to investigate, risking life and limb to solve the mystery before anyone else falls victim

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From the Hallelujah to the Weigh Down Diet
programme.desc: Xand van Tulleken and Helen Lawal oversee proceedings as three more pairs road test some of the most popular and talked about diets. Drag queens Jordan and Thomas have one week to drop a dress size ahead of their next performance, best friends Earl and Vince go on six-week faith-based diets to slim down before a forthcoming church celebration, and mums Maria and Jules want to lose weight for a kayaking trip in three months. Xand also investigates whether infrared can make people lighter and if it is possible to offset snacks by exercising at work

programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: In the final episode of the documentary series following the work of the Dyfed-Powys Police, Tenby celebrates the biggest event of the year - Ironman Wales. Managing thousands of visitors and the race is a challenge for the Harbour Cops

programme.title: Live League of Ireland
programme.desc: Sligo Rovers v Shamrock Rovers (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the Premier Division match at The Showgrounds. Shamrock are the reigning champions and will be looking to continue their recent dominance of the competition, while Sligo finished in seventh place last term and had a difficult start to the current campaign, failing to win any of their first three matches

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: The American version of the relationship-based reality show, in which singletons compete to pair off while living in a villa

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony-winning actor Viola Davis, star of Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 Will Poulter and renowned comedian Jack Dee. Plus, a performance from Simple Minds of their iconic hit Dont You (Forget About Me) ahead of the songs 40th anniversary

programme.title: You and Me
programme.desc: Ben finds his life changed for ever when catastrophe strikes on what should be the happiest day of his life. Romantic comedy drama told over two separate timelines, starring Harry Lawtey, Jessica Barden and Sophia Brown

programme.title: Tell Me Everything
programme.desc: Neves sister Timmi comes to stay while their parents are away, Jonny is avoiding Mei, and Neve is relieved that he is moving on - but events take a dramatic turn

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian and dog-lover meets some of the people who dedicate their lives to helping sick and abandoned canines in Delhi, where 400,000 pooches live on the streets. First up are a street pup with a potentially fatal virus and a golden retriever that will not stop chewing its paw. Paul also rolls up his sleeves like never before as he rescues a litter of puppies, born in the grounds of Lodhi Gardens, from almost certain death

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian helps more of the sick and abandoned dogs in Delhi. He meets a pup whose ears are infected and tries to train a boisterous labrador that cannot be rehomed because of its behavioural problems. He also goes out and about helping to treat some of the 400,000 waifs and strays on the citys streets

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The sports broadcaster talks to Tommy Martin about his childhood, his life as a footballer and his successful career as a journalist, radio host and TV pundit

programme.title: UEFA Champions League Magazine Show
programme.desc: A review of the latest matches in Europes premier club competition, plus a look ahead to forthcoming fixtures

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, three singletons compete in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of sweet shop worker Jessica Gill. To impress her, they have to dress her. Jess has no information to go on but the outfits bought, so which of the three competitors will she choose to take on a date?

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: Michelin-starred Paul Ainsworth takes part in the culinary challenge, putting three home cooks through their paces including a test of their butchery skills. Sheree Murphy presents

programme.title: Whos Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Brian McFadden is at the home of pop star and actor Adam Rickitt as he plays host to four strangers in his home. Will Adam avoid the washing up with his cryptic clues as to his identity or will the four diners bag the cash prize and hand the rubber gloves to him?

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Quiz, hosted by Omid Djalili, in which contestants Lance, Sophie, Elaine, Sean, Jake, Bridget, Nesta, Chidimma and Alex are assigned a number between one and nine. They are then whittled down to a final four, and the combination of numbers they are assigned represents the size of the jackpot they play to win in a final round

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Manchester, three singletons battle it out in a series of shopping challenges to win the affections of car sales executive Chelsea

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Among the UKs wealthiest 10 per cent, the Scaife-Lacy family from Beverley in East Yorkshire trade places with the Flinthams, who live just six miles away in Hull and are in the countrys poorest 10 per cent. They swap homes, budgets and lives for seven days as they see what it is like at the other end of the economic spectrum

programme.title: Cleaning Up Ireland
programme.desc: Street-cleaning twins Vinny and Pat tackle illegal dumping in Dublin. Paige and Shan blitz a luxury rental in Cork and decluttering duo Kim and Lyn tame a chaotic tween bedroom

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at the London station, meeting the army of workers who keep it running

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as comedian Russell Kane, This Mornings Dr Zoe Williams, broadcaster Kaye Adams, journalist and presenter Matt Allwright and writer and broadcaster Gyles Brandreth take on the Chasers in a bid to win big money for their chosen charities

programme.title: You Are What You Wear
programme.desc: Rylan hosts the fashion makeover show, with volunteers including a furniture restorer who has taken to wearing her husbands clothes, a budding Paralympian who is looking for a grown-up, wheelchair-friendly style and a hip-hop aficionado who wants to upgrade to a smarter street look. Finally, one of the stylists gets to work on a military wife and full-time mum who, after spending half her marriage living on army bases, says she has forgotten what fashion is

programme.title: Side Hustlers
programme.desc: Successful investors Emma Grede and Ashley Graham help tenacious female entrepreneurs transform their side hustles into investment-worthy main hustles - all in just 40 days

programme.desc: Three friends try to win fabulous prizes by defeating Rickie Haywood-Williams, Amy Dowden, Janine Harouni and Darren Harriott in a series of bizarre games

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: Paul helps a traumatised dog that desperately needs to find a home, and joins Delhis vets once more as they go out and about to treat injured street dogs. However, the pooches are not the only ones needing medical attention when the presenter falls ill

programme.title: Paul OGrady: For the Love of Dogs - India
programme.desc: The comedian and dog-lover presents his final report from Delhi, where 400,000 pooches live on the streets. Paul joins in with the celebrations for Diwali, the Hindu festival of light, with the people and animals of one of Delhis oldest dog shelters. He also helps a traumatised puppy make new friends, joins medics as they rush to rescue a four-week-old street pup who may have been hit by a car and falls in love with an elderly black labrador who lives at the rescue centre. Will he be able to resist bringing the pooch back to Britain with him?
